message.content:
You are an OPUS BUILDER creating (not yet fully running) the evidence-card quality AUDIT tool, per Sol's authoritative plan. A mine is running concurrently and will write the real cards (outputs/evidence_cards_v2.json) in ~40 min; your job is to BUILD + VALIDATE the audit so it launches instantly at mine-end.

PLAN FILE (read it first): /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/sota_review/foundation/SOL_CARD_AUDIT_PLAN.md
YOUR PHASE: ADVERSARY — build seed KNOWN-BAD cards (fabricated binding, CoT-contaminated claim, flipped numeric sign, off-topic) and assert the audit CATCHES each; then validate the whole pipeline end-to-end on the cards available NOW (outputs/recovered_table_cards.json if present, and a sample of outputs/evidence_cards_v2.json) — do a SMALL real opus run (a few cards) to prove the opus path works, then STOP (do not audit the full set — the real cards are still being mined)
ALREADY-BUILT PHASES: T0, HARNESS, DISPOSITION
REPO: /home/polaris/wt/flywheel

DO EXACTLY THIS:
1. Read /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/sota_review/foundation/SOL_CARD_AUDIT_PLAN.md fully. Build your phase's component exactly to Sol's spec — reuse report_ast/the entailment judge for faithfulness (do NOT reinvent), do NOT use the legacy scripts/quarantine.py (Sol forbids it — hardcodes journal-only). Put the audit code in a clear new module (e.g. scripts/card_audit/ or scripts/card_audit.py) — do NOT modify evidence_miner.py, provenance.py, event_ledger.py, report_ast.py, or the running mine.
2. Honor GENERALITY: no task-72 / no DOI/title/subject literals; rules fire on structure. Add the phase's test.
3. Run your phase's acceptance test; it must pass.
4. For the ADVERSARY phase: you MAY make a SMALL real opus call batch (a handful of cards) to prove the opus audit path works — but do NOT run the full audit; the real card set is still being mined.

HARD CONSTRAINTS:
- The mine (python evidence_miner.py --workers 24) and the recovery (recover_truncated_tables.py) are RUNNING. Do NOT touch them, their outputs (evidence_cards_v2.json, recovered_table_cards.json — you may READ these), outputs/blobs, or the ledger. Do NOT kill any process.
- The audit uses opus (Anthropic) — fine, different pool from the mine's OpenRouter. Set PYTHONPATH=scripts:src.
- Do NOT git commit.

Return the schema: built (bool), acceptance_passed (test exit 0), test_command, files_changed, one-paragraph summary, blocker (empty if none).

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
1	# Build plan: evidence-card corpus audit
2	
3	The audit will be a fail-closed, resumable offline workflow. Every serialized card receives deterministic verification and an independent Opus semantic audit. Every ATTRIBUTED card—currently all `evidence_cards_v2` records—receives two independent Opus passes. High-risk cases and disagreements receive a third adjudication. Nothing enters the composer unless every applicable dimension passes after any repair.
4	
5	One critical repo finding must be handled first: the current miner can consolidate unrelated qualitative cards as “corroboration,” omit binding fields from those nested records, and create duplicate card IDs. The audit cannot recover claims already erased by lossy consolidation. Phase 0 therefore includes an auditability gate and a mandatory replay branch if the final artifact exhibits that loss.
6	
7	## 1. Non-negotiable architecture
8	
9	Use these existing enforcement points:
10	
11	- Faithfulness: [`report_ast.entailed_by_span()`](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/report_ast.py:1106).
12	- Card-to-binding adapter: [`report_ast._binding_from_card()`](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/report_ast.py:199).
13	- Binding verification: [`Graph.verify_span()`](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/provenance.py:1454).
14	- Binding-specific source-policy resolution: [`Graph.resolve_attribution(binding, policy)`](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/provenance.py:1311).
15	- Whole critical path: [`CardBundle.resolve()`](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/report_ast.py:356).
16	- Typed OWNED validation: [`report_ast.validate_node()`](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/report_ast.py:1493).
17	- Card claim reconstruction: [`evidence_miner.derive_claim()`](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/evidence_miner.py:1254).
18	- Act rules: [`config/evidence_acts.json`](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/config/evidence_acts.json).
19	- Atomic artifact pattern: [`publisher._atomic_write()`](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/publisher.py:108).
20	
21	Do not use legacy [`scripts/quarantine.py`](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/quarantine.py). It hardcodes journal-only policy and resolves manifestation-wide rather than binding-specific.
22	
23	The workflow follows an audit-then-adjudicate pattern: a failure or disagreement must carry exact evidence and can be revised only through an explicit adjudication/repair record. This is consistent with the 2026 DeepFact “Audit-then-Score” finding that difficult factuality labels become more reliable through evidence-backed challenge and adjudication rather than one-shot judgment. [DeepFact, ACL 2026](https://aclanthology.org/2026.acl-long.1586/)
24	
25	## 2. Exact per-card pass/fail contract
26	
27	Use four verdict values internally:
28	
29	- `PASS`
30	- `FAIL`
31	- `UNCERTAIN`
32	- `NOT_APPLICABLE`
33	
34	No `UNCERTAIN` may appear in the composer-facing set.
35	
36	### Faithfulness
37	
38	For each top-level card and each claimed corroborating support edge:
39	
40	1. Resolve the exact raw binding.
41	2. Obtain `resolved_span = manifestation.text[span_start:span_end]`.
42	3. Normalize the claim exactly as the composer does: HTML-unescape, collapse whitespace, strip only a final period.
43	4. Call:
44	
45	```python
46	report_ast.entailed_by_span(
47	    normalized_claim,
48	    resolved_span,
49	    graph.works[manifestation.work_id],
50	)
51	```
52	
53	Pass only if the result is `(True, "")` or equivalent successful return.
54	
55	Precise rules:
56	
57	- `ATTRIBUTED`: every factual atom must be entailed by its own bound span.
58	- `OWNED`: not tested for source entailment, but must pass the report-AST OWNED gate and carry no new particular.
59	- A corroborating span must independently entail the primary claim. It never inherits the primary card’s pass.
60	- `NOT_ENTAILED`, `UNCERTAIN`, transport failure, malformed judge output, or missing span means `FAIL`.
61	- A report-AST failure cannot be overridden by an Opus opinion. The card must be repaired and rerun.
62	- Never test against `card["span"]`, concatenated metadata, wider context, another source, or a corroboration basket. Use only verified `span_raw` bytes.
63	- Never concatenate `span + claim`; that recreates evidence laundering.
64	
65	### CoT contamination
66	
67	This is a positive structural test, not a blacklist of phrases.
68	
69	Every non-empty string field must be classified into exactly one allowed provenance class:
70	
71	- `SOURCE_BYTES`: exact verified source substring, such as `span_raw`.
72	- `CANONICAL_SOURCE_VIEW`: deterministic normalization of verified source bytes, such as `span`.
73	- `DERIVED_CACHE`: byte-for-byte recomputable from audited fields, such as `claim`.
74	- `GRAPH_METADATA`: exactly matches live Work/Expression/Manifestation metadata.
75	- `REGISTRY_VALUE`: member of an allowed closed vocabulary.
76	- `ATOMIC_EVIDENCE_VALUE`: concise extracted value semantically supported by its declared source window.
77	- `EMPTY`.
78	
79	Fail if any string is:
80	
81	- process narration;
82	- model self-talk;
83	- instructions to itself or another model;
84	- analysis of how to answer;
85	- candidate enumeration or abandoned alternatives;
86	- confidence commentary;
87	- JSON/prompt scaffolding;
88	- an unclassified free-text field;
89	- a derived cache that does not exactly recompute.
90	
91	Opus must return a content class for every non-empty auditable field. Absence of suspicious phrases is never sufficient.
92	
93	A source span that itself says “we reason…” remains `SOURCE_BYTES`; that is author prose, not leaked miner CoT. If apparent CoT text cannot be proven to be source bytes, it fails.
94	
95	### Numeric fidelity
96	
97	Run both report-AST mechanical checks and an Opus atom-level comparison.
98	
99	All subdimensions must pass:
100	
101	- `number`: same canonical number, including sign and decimal precision.
102	- `direction`: rose/fell, positive/negative, increase/decrease unchanged.
103	- `magnitude`: no doubled/halved/substantial/majority or similar inflation.
104	- `unit`: percent and percentage points remain distinct; currency, rate, time, and denominator preserved.
105	- `comparator`: baseline, control group, “per,” “relative to,” and reference category preserved.
106	- `population`: no widening from subgroup to all people/firms/studies.
107	- `geography`: no local-to-global widening.
108	- `period`: dates, follow-up interval, forecast horizon, and temporal qualifiers preserved.
109	- `scope`: technology, industry, unit of analysis, outcome, and conditions preserved.
110	- `uncertainty`: confidence interval, p-value, approximation, “at least,” “up to,” null result, mixed result, and exceptions preserved.
111	- `modality`: association is not upgraded to causation; forecast is not rendered as observation.
112	- `precision`: claim may not add digits, remove uncertainty, or invent exactness.
113	
114	Any failed atom means the dimension fails. A mechanical pass is never an admission; semantic judgment remains required.
115	
116	### Relevance and facet correctness
117	
118	The audit must use the exact pinned research question and frozen compiled contract.
119	
120	Pass relevance only when Opus classifies the card as:
121	
122	- `DIRECT_ANSWER_EVIDENCE`, or
123	- `NECESSARY_CONTEXT` with a specific connection to the research question.
124	
125	Fail:
126	
127	- merely adjacent subject matter;
128	- generic filler;
129	- evidence useful to a different question;
130	- source metadata or methods with no role in answering the question;
131	- a span whose only relevant content exists outside its binding.
132	
133	Facet rules:
134	
135	- Every existing `facet_tags_span` value must be supported by the span.
136	- Context-derived tags must be supported by the declared context window.
137	- Every tag must exist in the pinned contract taxonomy.
138	- A relevant card with incorrect tags is repairable by retagging.
139	- A semantically off-topic card is quarantined; its source remains in the corpus.
140	- If the question or exact contract cannot be pinned, the audit stops. It must not invent a fallback topic.
141	
142	### Structure and binding
143	
144	Pass only if all are true:
145	
146	- JSON type and closed schema are valid.
147	- Required fields exist and have valid types.
148	- `id` is non-empty and globally unique in the clean set.
149	- `manifestation_id`, hash, offsets, and `span_raw` pass `Graph.verify_span`.
150	- Stored `permitted_expression_ids` exactly equal the live per-span set.
151	- Binding-specific `resolve_attribution(binding, derived_policy)` admits.
152	- Stored `attribution_target_expression_id` equals the live target.
153	- `expression_id == manifestation.expression_id`.
154	- `work_id == evidence_unit_id == manifestation.work_id`.
155	- Stored source policy equals the policy re-derived from the original question.
156	- The manifestation’s `semantic_binding` is in `IDENTITY_PROVEN`.
157	- The `(semantic_binding, expression_kind)` pair is in `COMPATIBLE_VERSION_PAIRS`.
158	- Work DOI/authors/year/venue caches agree with live graph metadata.
159	- `source_version`, `text_field`, aliases, and normalized span agree with live values.
160	- `act` exists in the pinned act registry and required/allowed fields comply.
161	- `claim == derive_claim(card, act)` after canonical normalization.
162	- `has_number`, `span_numbers`, `complete_tuple`, aliases, and source counts recompute exactly.
163	- Every nested corroborator has a complete independent binding.
164	- `n_sources` and `n_evidence_units` equal verified independent evidence units, not stored counts.
165	
166	Unknown fields fail schema validation until explicitly added to the schema.
167	
168	### Voice
169	
170	The current v2 schema has no voice field. Therefore:
171	
172	- Every incoming v2 evidence card is normalized as `ATTRIBUTED`.
173	- A failing card must never be silently reinterpreted as `OWNED`.
174	- `card_kind` is not voice.
175	- Only an explicit repair disposition can create an OWNED suggestion, and it is not citeable evidence.
176	
177	## 3. Tiering and Opus coverage
178	
179	### Tier 0: deterministic screen on everything
180	
181	Run on:
182	
183	- every top-level record;
184	- every primary binding;
185	- every `corroborating_sources` entry;
186	- every `same_unit_other_expressions` entry for structure, though these do not support the claim;
187	- every non-empty card field.
188	
189	This handles byte hashes, offsets, schema, caches, numeric token checks, duplicate IDs, source-policy resolution, and registry consistency.
190	
191	### Tier 1: first Opus pass on every card
192	
193	Every top-level card receives a complete Opus judgment covering:
194	
195	- faithfulness;
196	- CoT content classes for every field;
197	- numeric atoms;
198	- relevance;
199	- facet correctness;
200	- proposed disposition.
201	
202	Every primary and corroborating claim/span pair is examined. Structurally unreachable cards still receive the remaining semantic judgments; their faithfulness verdict is `UNREACHABLE`.
203	
204	There is no sample-only lane.
205	
206	### Tier 2: second independent Opus pass
207	
208	Run in a fresh context with no access to the first verdict for:
209	
210	- every ATTRIBUTED card;
211	- every numeric card;
212	- every low-corroboration card;
213	- every card with a non-span field used in its claim;
214	- forecasts, recommendations, null results, limitations, mixed findings, and third-party attribution cues;
215	- every report-AST failure or uncertainty;
216	- every card flagged for CoT, relevance, facet, or numeric risk.
217	
218	Since the current v2 corpus is entirely ATTRIBUTED, all current cards receive two independent Opus passes.
219	
220	### Tier 3: adjudication
221	
222	A third Opus agent receives the card, exact source bytes, deterministic receipt, report-AST result, and both independent Opus verdicts when:
223	
224	- the Opus passes disagree;
225	- either Opus pass fails;
226	- report-AST passes but Opus alleges an unsupported atom;
227	- a repair is proposed;
228	- a support edge is proposed for removal;
229	- a card is proposed for OWNED demotion.
230	
231	The adjudicator must identify the deciding source substring and atom. It may resolve an Opus false positive, but it cannot override a failed byte binding or failed `entailed_by_span`. Those require a new repaired card and a complete rerun.
232	
233	### Efficient execution
234	
235	Configure, do not hardcode:
236	
237	- first-pass packets: token-bounded to approximately 12,000 input tokens and at most 16 cards;
238	- second-pass packets: at most 8 cards;
239	- adjudication and repair: one card per packet;
240	- default concurrency: 24 Opus worker processes;
241	- maximum retries: 3, same model only;
242	- no fallback to Sonnet, Haiku, GLM, or a deterministic “pass.”
243	
244	The orchestrator bin-packs by actual serialized length so long spans or large corroboration sets receive smaller batches.
245	
246	Use fresh `claude -p` sessions with:
247	
248	```text
249	--model opus
250	--effort max
251	--permission-mode dontAsk
252	--output-format json
253	--json-schema <audit schema>
254	```
255	
256	Workers get read-only, self-contained packets and no Agent, Bash, Edit, Write, web, or task-spawning tools. Require response metadata to prove the requested model was used.
257	
258	Opus output must be short structured receipts, not its reasoning trace.
259	
260	## 4. Disposition rules
261	
262	Never delete silently. Every input row and support edge gets exactly one final disposition.
263	
264	### `KEEP_UNCHANGED`
265	
266	All applicable dimensions pass.
267	
268	### `REPAIR_TIGHTEN`
269	
270	Allowed when the span supports a narrower claim.
271	
272	Examples:
273	
274	- delete an unsupported magnitude adjective;
275	- narrow population or geography;
276	- restore uncertainty;
277	- correct an optional tuple field;
278	- remove a contaminated optional field;
279	- correct facet tags.
280	
281	Rules:
282	
283	1. Repair agent proposes typed field changes, not arbitrary replacement prose.
284	2. Source bytes, hash, manifestation, and offsets remain immutable.
285	3. Recompute the claim using `derive_claim`; do not accept model-written claim prose.
286	4. Rerun every deterministic dimension.
287	5. Rerun report-AST entailment.
288	6. Require two fresh Opus passes.
289	7. Record before/after hashes and exact changed fields.
290	8. Original remains in the lineage ledger as `SUPERSEDED`.
291	
292	### `REBASE_TO_VALID_SUPPORT`
293	
294	If the primary span fails but a corroborating source independently supports the claim:
295	
296	- rebuild a complete binding from that source’s graph bytes;
297	- make it the primary;
298	- issue a collision-safe card ID;
299	- recompute attribution and counts;
300	- rerun all dimensions.
301	
302	Never merely swap the attribution string.
303	
304	### `REMOVE_BAD_SUPPORT_EDGE`
305	
306	If the primary card passes but one corroborator does not:
307	
308	- retain the primary card;
309	- move the bad edge into quarantine with its reason;
310	- recompute `n_sources` and `n_evidence_units`;
311	- rerun low-corroboration/risk classification.
312	
313	The disappearance must be counted as a repair, never hidden.
314	
315	### `DEMOTE_TO_OWNED_SUGGESTION`
316	
317	Sound only when the replacement:
318	
319	- contains no number, named source, unsupported entity, magnitude, particular population, or source-specific finding;
320	- is a genuine frame, transition, or proof-carrying synthesis;
321	- for synthesis, names at least two passing premise IDs;
322	- passes `report_ast.validate_node(Owned(...), bundle)`.
323	
324	An overclaimed numerical magnitude cannot be demoted while retaining the number. It must be tightened or quarantined.
325	
326	Because evidence cards are citeable ATTRIBUTED objects, demoted material moves to `owned_suggestions.json`; it does not remain in `audited_cards.json`. This preserves it without laundering it through the evidence lane.
327	
328	### `QUARANTINE_CARD`
329	
330	Required for:
331	
332	- invalid or unverifiable binding;
333	- unresolved or wrong identity;
334	- inadmissible expression under the derived source policy;
335	- no entailed repair;
336	- unremovable CoT contamination in a required field;
337	- numeric contradiction;
338	- off-topic card;
339	- irreparable schema ambiguity;
340	- duplicate-ID collision that cannot be safely re-ID’d;
341	- consolidation information loss.
342	
343	### `QUARANTINE_SUPPORT_EDGE`
344	
345	Used for false corroboration or incomplete nested bindings when the primary remains valid.
346	
347	There is no `DELETE` disposition.
348	
349	## 5. Agent-executable phased implementation
350	
351	## Phase 0 — Freeze the completed mine and prove auditability
352	
353	Files:
354	
355	- New: `scripts/evidence_card_audit.py`
356	- New: `src/schemas/evidence_card_audit.py`
357	- Modify when necessary: [`scripts/evidence_miner.py`](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/evidence_miner.py:3151)
358	- Inputs:
359	  - `outputs/evidence_cards_v2.json`
360	  - `outputs/evidence_cards_v2.meta.json`
361	  - `outputs/evidence_cards_v2.quarantine.json`
362	  - `outputs/journal_corpus_content.json`
363	  - `outputs/provenance_graph.json`
364	  - exact contract under `outputs/contracts/`
365	  - `outputs/event_ledger.jsonl`
366	  - `config/evidence_acts.json`
367	
368	Exact steps:
369	
370	1. Wait for the miner process to exit successfully. Do not infer completion from file existence; the current on-disk 521-card file is stale until `mine()` returns.
371	2. Read each input twice and require identical SHA-256 values.
372	3. Copy inputs into:
373	
374	```text
375	outputs/audits/evidence_cards/<cards_sha>/inputs/
376	```
377	
378	4. Write `input_manifest.json` containing:
379	   - absolute original paths;
380	   - size and SHA-256;
381	   - card count;
382	   - git commit;
383	   - question;
384	   - exact source policy;
385	   - exact contract path/hash;
386	   - graph hash;
387	   - corpus hash;
388	   - act-registry hash/version.
389	5. Strict-load the graph with `Graph.from_json`.
390	6. Re-derive policy from `meta.question`; compare with metadata and every card.
391	7. Pin the exact cached research contract. Do not recompile it.
392	8. Reconstruct the graph from the pinned corpus if the miner did not persist its exact graph. Require every card binding to verify against the chosen graph.
393	9. Enumerate top-level rows by `audit_row_id = sha256(input_sha + JSON pointer + canonical row JSON)` so duplicate card IDs cannot hide records.
394	10. Enumerate every nested support edge separately.
395	11. Test for:
396	    - duplicate card IDs;
397	    - unrelated qualitative records merged as corroboration;
398	    - nested corroborators missing `span_raw` or permitted IDs;
399	    - raw-card information loss;
400	    - mismatch between `cards_pre_consolidation` and serialized accounting.
401	
402	Acceptance:
403	
404	- `input_manifest.json` is complete and immutable.
405	- All top-level rows and nested edges have stable audit IDs.
406	- Graph and contract are uniquely pinned.
407	- No input changes after snapshot.
408	- Auditability status is `PASS`.
409	
410	### Mandatory Phase 0R branch — repair lossy miner serialization
411	
412	Enter this branch if unrelated qualitative consolidation or unrecoverable nested cards are found.
413	
414	Modify [`scripts/evidence_miner.py`](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/evidence_miner.py):
415	
416	1. Write `evidence_cards_v2.raw.jsonl` atomically before consolidation.
417	2. Make qualitative `finding_key()` include:
418	   - act;
419	   - canonical primary content field (`finding`, `holding`, `recommendation`, or `limitation`);
420	   - outcome/scope where applicable.
421	3. Make IDs include a stable act/claim hash in addition to binding coordinates.
422	4. Extend `_binding_of()` to preserve complete binding fields plus the nested card’s own claim and typed fields.
423	5. Persist exact graph, contract, corpus, prompt/model/version, and hashes in miner metadata.
424	6. Write cards/meta/quarantine through atomic replacement.
425	7. Replay the mine from a saved raw checkpoint. If none exists, rerun the mine and disclose the extra run.
426	
427	Acceptance:
428	
429	- Raw-card accounting is exact.
430	- No duplicate clean IDs.
431	- No unrelated qualitative card is labeled corroboration.
432	- Every nested binding independently resolves.
433	- The completed replay becomes the new pinned Phase 0 input.
434	- The original completed mine remains archived, not overwritten.
435	
436	If Phase 0R is required and not completed, the workflow must not claim that every mined card was audited.
437	
438	## Phase 1 — Deterministic inventory and screen
439	
440	Files:
441	
442	- `scripts/evidence_card_audit.py`
443	- `src/schemas/evidence_card_audit.py`
444	- New: `config/settings/evidence_card_audit.yaml`
445	- Tests: `tests/test_evidence_card_audit.py`
446	
447	Exact steps:
448	
449	1. Define closed schemas for:
450	   - input card;
451	   - support edge;
452	   - deterministic receipt;
453	   - Opus verdict;
454	   - adjudication;
455	   - repair;
456	   - disposition;
457	   - audit manifest.
458	2. Normalize all v2 rows as `voice=ATTRIBUTED`.
459	3. Run structure/binding rules against every row/edge.
460	4. Recompute all derived caches and counts.
461	5. Run report-AST mechanical and semantic entailment on every ATTRIBUTED claim/support pair.
462	6. Store append-only receipts in:
463	   - `inventory.jsonl`
464	   - `deterministic_receipts.jsonl`
465	   - `entailment_receipts.jsonl`
466	7. Continue evaluating other dimensions after a hard failure so the adversary battery can show every planted fault was detected.
467	
468	Acceptance:
469	
470	- Inventory count equals the full serialized census.
471	- Every audit ID has one deterministic receipt.
472	- Every ATTRIBUTED support edge has one report-AST receipt.
473	- Missing/uncertain judge results are failures.
474	- Reopening receipts and rerunning produces identical decisions.
475	
476	## Phase 2 — Opus prompt and structured verdict contract
477	
478	Files:
479	
480	- New: `config/prompts/evidence_card_audit_opus.md`
481	- New: `config/prompts/evidence_card_adjudicator_opus.md`
482	- New: `config/prompts/evidence_card_repair_opus.md`
483	- `src/schemas/evidence_card_audit.py`
484	- Tests: `tests/test_evidence_card_audit_generality.py`
485	
486	Exact steps:
487	
488	1. Prompts accept only injected values:
489	   - research question;
490	   - frozen contract facets;
491	   - card fields;
492	   - exact verified span;
493	   - support role;
494	   - deterministic receipt.
495	2. Require per-dimension JSON objects with:
496	   - verdict;
497	   - machine reason code;
498	   - unsupported atom;
499	   - deciding source substring;
500	   - affected field;
501	   - proposed disposition.
502	3. Require a CoT content class for every non-empty field.
503	4. For numeric claims, require all numeric subdimension verdicts.
504	5. Do not include task-72 subjects, benchmark examples, AI/labor-market vocabulary, DOI literals, venue literals, or a fixed journal-only policy.
505	6. Validate every agent response against JSON schema before accepting it.
506	
507	Acceptance:
508	
509	- The prompts work unchanged on clinical, legal, economics, and computer-science fixtures.
510	- Unknown/missing fields fail schema validation.
511	- No free-form agent reasoning is persisted.
512	- Opus cannot mark a structurally unreachable span faithful.
513	
514	## Phase 3 — Durable, parallel Opus orchestrator
515	
516	Files:
517	
518	- New: `scripts/evidence_card_audit_orchestrator.py`
519	- `config/settings/evidence_card_audit.yaml`
520	- Reference pattern: [`scripts/autoloop/orchestrator.py`](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/autoloop/orchestrator.py)
521	- Reference watchdog: [`scripts/iarch007_box_watchdog.sh`](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/iarch007_box_watchdog.sh)
522	
523	Exact steps:
524	
525	1. Build token-bounded batches from inventory.
526	2. Persist each request before invocation:
527	
528	```text
529	batches/<pass>/<batch_id>.request.json
530	```
531	
532	3. Launch bounded parallel `claude -p --model opus --effort max` workers.
533	4. Write responses via temp file, fsync, and `os.replace`.
534	5. Validate:
535	   - response schema;
536	   - every requested audit ID appears exactly once;
537	   - no foreign ID appears;
538	   - model metadata proves Opus;
539	   - response is complete.
540	6. Append accepted verdicts to `opus_pass_a.jsonl` or `opus_pass_b.jsonl`.
541	7. Maintain `progress.json` with:
542	   - total/completed/failed/pending batches;
543	   - last heartbeat;
544	   - current worker PIDs;
545	   - retry count;
546	   - token/cost telemetry.
547	8. `--resume` reconstructs state exclusively from durable files.
548	9. Watchdog relaunches a dead orchestrator at most three times with `--resume`.
549	10. Retry provider failures with the same model. Never degrade.
550	
551	Acceptance:
552	
553	- Killing the orchestrator midway and restarting produces no duplicate or missing verdicts.
554	- Batch order and worker completion order do not affect canonical output.
555	- Every top-level card has pass A.
556	- Every ATTRIBUTED card has pass B.
557	- No batch is considered complete from an unvalidated response.
558	
559	## Phase 4 — Adjudication and repair
560	
561	Files:
562	
563	- `scripts/evidence_card_audit.py`
564	- `scripts/evidence_card_audit_orchestrator.py`
565	- Opus adjudicator/repair prompts
566	- Tests: `tests/test_evidence_card_audit.py`
567	
568	Exact steps:
569	
570	1. Join deterministic, report-AST, Opus A, and Opus B receipts by audit ID.
571	2. Generate one-card adjudication packets for disagreements or failures.
572	3. Apply fail-closed combination rules.
573	4. Generate typed repair proposals only for repairable cases.
574	5. Apply repairs in a new object; never mutate the frozen input.
575	6. Recompute claim through `derive_claim`.
576	7. Rerun the entire audit stack on repaired cards.
577	8. Record:
578	   - original hash;
579	   - repaired hash;
580	   - changed fields;
581	   - repair agent;
582	   - adjudicator;
583	   - old/new verdict vectors.
584	9. Emit final disposition for every row and support edge.
585	
586	Acceptance:
587	
588	```text
589	input_top_level
590	  = kept_unchanged
591	  + repaired_and_superseded
592	  + quarantined
593	  + demoted_to_owned_suggestion
594	```
595	
596	And separately:
597	
598	```text
599	input_support_edges
600	  = kept_support_edges
601	  + repaired_support_edges
602	  + quarantined_support_edges
603	```
604	
605	No unaccounted row or edge is allowed.
606	
607	## Phase 5 — Publish clean set and quarantine ledger
608	
609	Files:
610	
611	- `scripts/evidence_card_audit.py`
612	- Reuse `publisher._atomic_write()`
613	- Outputs under the SHA-addressed audit directory
614	
615	Exact steps:
616	
617	1. Build `audited_cards.json` from `KEEP_UNCHANGED`, validated repairs, and valid rebases only.
618	2. Build:
619	   - `quarantine.json`
620	   - `repairs.json`
621	   - `owned_suggestions.json`
622	   - `decisions.jsonl`
623	3. Sort outputs canonically.
624	4. Reopen the clean set and construct `CardBundle`.
625	5. Run `cellcog_composer.reverify(bundle)`.
626	6. Require zero duplicate IDs and zero failed primary/corroborating resolutions.
627	7. Rerun `entailed_by_span` over every shipped claim/support edge.
628	8. Atomically publish:
629	   - `outputs/evidence_cards_audited.json`
630	   - `outputs/evidence_cards_audited.manifest.json`
631	9. Recompute hashes from disk after publication.
632	
633	Acceptance:
634	
635	- Every shipped card passes all applicable dimensions.
636	- Every shipped support edge passes independently.
637	- No `UNCERTAIN`, `FAIL`, or missing receipt exists in the clean set.
638	- Clean file SHA matches both manifests.
639	- Raw v2 is unchanged.
640	- Quarantine and repair ledgers preserve every excluded/superseded object.
641	
642	## Phase 6 — Composer interlock
643	
644	Files:
645	
646	- Modify [`scripts/cellcog_composer.py`](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/cellcog_composer.py:144)
647	- Tests:
648	  - `tests/test_evidence_card_audit.py`
649	  - existing release/binding tests
650	
651	Exact steps:
652	
653	1. Remove the production default to `evidence_cards_bound.json`.
654	2. Require:
655	   - `--cards outputs/evidence_cards_audited.json`
656	   - `--audit-manifest <path>`
657	   - exact graph path;
658	   - exact ledger path;
659	   - expected cards/graph hashes.
660	3. In `load_bundle`, verify:
661	   - audit manifest status is `COMPLETE`;
662	   - planted adversary status is `PASS`;
663	   - zero unresolved decisions;
664	   - clean-set SHA;
665	   - graph/corpus/contract hashes;
666	   - derived source policy.
667	4. Derive policy from the manifest/question; do not default production to journal-only.
668	5. Preserve `CardBundle.resolve()` and `reverify()` before any composer LLM call.
669	6. Stop overwriting a supplied contract with `AP.default_contract()`.
670	7. Build jobs and planner facets from the pinned actual contract.
671	8. Ensure publisher provenance records the audit-manifest hash.
672	
673	Acceptance:
674	
675	- Raw `evidence_cards_v2.json` is rejected as a production composer input.
676	- Missing/incomplete/tampered audit manifest is rejected before any LLM call.
677	- A one-byte clean-set edit is rejected.
678	- A source-policy mismatch is rejected.
679	- Composer `--dry` succeeds on the exact audited bundle with zero binding failures.
680	
681	## Phase 7 — Audit report
682	
683	Files:
684	
685	- New outputs:
686	  - `audit_report.json`
687	  - `audit_report.md`
688	  - `worst_offenders.json`
689	
690	Required metrics:
691	
692	- Input census:
693	  - top-level cards;
694	  - primary bindings;
695	  - corroborating edges;
696	  - same-unit expression records.
697	- Per-dimension raw pass/fail/uncertain/N-A counts and rates.
698	- Post-repair pass rates.
699	- Report-AST vs Opus A/B agreement matrix.
700	- Opus A/B agreement rate.
701	- Adjudication count and outcomes.
702	- Quarantine counts by stable reason code.
703	- Repairs by type.
704	- Demotions by type.
705	- False corroboration removed.
706	- Duplicate-ID collisions.
707	- Consolidation information loss.
708	- Pass/quarantine by:
709	  - Work and manifestation;
710	  - act;
711	  - section;
712	  - facet;
713	  - source policy;
714	  - expression kind;
715	  - numeric/non-numeric;
716	  - corroboration level.
717	- Worst offenders:
718	  - highest failure count;
719	  - highest failure rate with denominator;
720	  - severe fabrication examples;
721	  - CoT-contaminated fields;
722	  - numeric/sign errors;
723	  - off-topic clusters.
724	- Coverage impact:
725	  - cards and independent evidence units retained per facet;
726	  - lost facets;
727	  - low-corroboration areas.
728	- Model, prompt, code, input, and output hashes.
729	- Tokens, cost, wall time, retries, and provider failures.
730	
731	Report both raw and post-repair rates. Never make a repaired corpus appear as if the raw mine had passed.
732	
733	Acceptance:
734	
735	- JSON and Markdown totals reconcile exactly with decisions.
736	- Every reported offender links to audit IDs and exact receipts.
737	- No sample-based claim is presented as corpus-wide.
738	- `composer_ready=true` only when the clean-set and adversary gates pass.
739	
740	## Phase 8 — Generality, metamorphic, and adversarial acceptance
741	
742	Files:
743	
744	- New: `tests/test_evidence_card_audit_adversary.py`
745	- New: `tests/test_evidence_card_audit_generality.py`
746	- New fixtures under:
747	  - `tests/fixtures/evidence_card_audit/`
748	- Existing regression suites remain mandatory.
749	
750	### Metamorphic test
751	
752	Create structurally identical fixtures in:
753	
754	- clinical medicine;
755	- case law;
756	- economics;
757	- computer science.
758	
759	Change only:
760	
761	- question subject;
762	- Work identifiers;
763	- authors and venues;
764	- facet vocabulary.
765	
766	Assert the verdict vector and disposition are unchanged.
767	
768	Additional metamorphic assertions:
769	
770	- renaming IDs changes no verdict;
771	- input-order permutation changes no verdict or final canonical SHA;
772	- changing batch boundaries changes no verdict;
773	- journal-only versus any-version wording changes only source-policy admissibility;
774	- adding one unsupported magnitude changes only the appropriate faithfulness/numeric result.
775	
776	### Planted adversary
777	
778	After snapshotting, create a test-only copy and seed one compound known-bad card with:
779	
780	- a fabricated or tampered binding;
781	- CoT text in the claim/field;
782	- a flipped direction;
783	- off-topic content.
784	
785	Run every dimension even after the first failure and require:
786	
787	- structure catches the fabricated binding;
788	- CoT catches the contaminated field;
789	- numeric fidelity catches the sign flip;
790	- relevance catches off-topic content;
791	- final disposition is quarantine;
792	- it never appears in `audited_cards.json`.
793	
794	Also add four single-fault cards so one failure cannot mask another.
795	
796	Additional attacks:
797	
798	- valid hash but reversed/empty offsets;
799	- wrong permitted expression set;
800	- source-policy laundering;
801	- unknown semantic-binding verdict;
802	- impossible semantic-binding/expression-kind pair;
803	- duplicate card ID;
804	- false corroborating span;
805	- invented precision;
806	- percent/percentage-point swap;
807	- association-to-causation upgrade;
808	- subgroup-to-global scope widening;
809	- judge exception, timeout, garbage, or uncertainty;
810	- a side-judge `("ENTAILED", "judge_error: ...")` sentinel, which must map to uncertainty rather than admission;
811	- contaminated optional field repair;
812	- attempted ATTRIBUTED-to-OWNED laundering.
813	
814	Acceptance:
815	
816	- The compound canary is caught in all four requested dimensions.
817	- Every single-fault canary is caught by its intended dimension.
818	- No planted fabrication survives.
819	- Removing or disabling any critical check makes at least one adversary test fail.
820	- No task-specific literal appears in production audit prompts or rules.
821	
822	## 6. Regression commands
823	
824	Run focused tests first:
825	
826	```bash
827	cd /home/polaris/wt/flywheel
828	PYTHONPATH=scripts:src pytest -q \
829	  tests/test_evidence_card_audit.py \
830	  tests/test_evidence_card_audit_adversary.py \
831	  tests/test_evidence_card_audit_generality.py
832	```
833	
834	Then the existing binding and policy battery:
835	
836	```bash
837	PYTHONPATH=scripts:src pytest -q \
838	  tests/test_binding_gate_foundation.py \
839	  tests/test_binding_gate_acceptance.py \
840	  tests/test_binding_gate_adversary.py \
841	  tests/test_correspondence_semantic_identity.py \
842	  tests/test_mining_identity_preskip.py \
843	  tests/test_source_policy_derivation.py \
844	  tests/test_version_derivation_unification.py
845	```
846	
847	Then production-path attacks:
848	
849	```bash
850	PYTHONPATH=scripts:src python scripts/evidence_miner.py --self-test
851	PYTHONPATH=scripts:src python scripts/test_gate_is_wired.py
852	PYTHONPATH=scripts:src python scripts/test_fabrication_paths.py
853	PYTHONPATH=scripts:src python scripts/test_release_e2e_attack.py
854	```
855	
856	Final dry composer invocation:
857	
858	```bash
859	python -u scripts/cellcog_composer.py \
860	  --dry \
861	  --cards outputs/evidence_cards_audited.json \
862	  --audit-manifest outputs/evidence_cards_audited.manifest.json \
863	  --graph <pinned-graph-path> \
864	  --ledger <pinned-ledger-path> \
865	  --policy <manifest-derived-policy> \
866	  --expect-cards-sha <audited-cards-sha> \
867	  --expect-graph-sha <graph-sha>
868	```
869	
870	## 7. Production run sequence
871	
872	1. Wait for mine exit.
873	2. Snapshot and hash inputs.
874	3. Run Phase 0 auditability gate.
875	4. If necessary, execute Phase 0R and replay the mine.
876	5. Run deterministic inventory.
877	6. Run report-AST entailment receipts.
878	7. Launch Opus A across every card.
879	8. Launch Opus B across every ATTRIBUTED/high-risk card.
880	9. Launch adjudication and repair jobs.
881	10. Rerun all repaired cards from zero.
882	11. Run the planted adversary against a test copy.
883	12. Build clean/quarantine/repair/owned artifacts.
884	13. Reopen and independently reverify the clean set.
885	14. Publish audited cards and manifest atomically.
886	15. Run composer dry interlock.
887	16. Mark manifest `COMPLETE` and `composer_ready=true`.
888	
889	The main session only monitors `progress.json`, worker health, and the five-minute forensic log. It does not make card-level judgments or manually advance batches.
890	
891	## Definition of done
892	
893	The audit is complete only when:
894	
895	- 100% of serialized cards and support edges are accounted for;
896	- any lossy miner serialization has been repaired and replayed;
897	- every current v2 card has two independent Opus verdicts;
898	- every shipped ATTRIBUTED claim passes `report_ast.entailed_by_span`;
899	- every shipped binding resolves under the pinned derived policy;
900	- every shipped numeric atom matches its source;
901	- every shipped field has positive clean-content provenance;
902	- every shipped card is relevant and correctly facet-tagged;
903	- no unresolved or ambiguous decision remains;
904	- quarantine is complete and counted;
905	- the known-bad card is caught and absent from the clean output;
906	- `CardBundle` and composer dry-run accept the exact audited artifact;
907	- the composer cannot consume raw or unaudited cards.
