147	# =================================================================================================
148	# The typed repair proposal — TYPED field changes only, never replacement claim prose (Sol §REPAIR 1)
149	# =================================================================================================
150	@dataclass
151	class RepairProposal:
152	    """What a repair agent (or the deterministic edge logic) proposes for ONE card. It is TYPED: a set of
153	    field values to change and a set of fields to remove — NOT free replacement prose (Sol §REPAIR_TIGHTEN
154	    1). The claim and every derived cache are recomputed by the engine, never taken from here. For a
155	    DEMOTE, `owned_text`/`owned_premise_ids` carry the reviewer-voice replacement the OWNED gate validates.
156	    For a REBASE, `rebase_edge_index` names the corroborator to promote (or None to let the engine pick the
157	    first independently-supporting one)."""
158	    field_changes: dict = field(default_factory=dict)
159	    remove_fields: tuple[str, ...] = ()
160	    rebase_edge_index: int | None = None
161	    owned_text: str = ''
162	    owned_premise_ids: tuple[str, ...] = ()
163	
164	
165	def validate_repair_proposal(proposal: RepairProposal) -> list[str]:
166	    """Refuse a repair that touches an immutable byte/identity field, a recomputed derived cache, or an
167	    unknown field (Sol §REPAIR_TIGHTEN 2-3). Returns the reason codes for every illegal touch; empty when
168	    the typed changes are all legal repairable fields."""
169	    from card_audit.audit_schema import TOP_LEVEL_FIELDS
170	    rcs: list[str] = []
171	    touched = set(proposal.field_changes) | set(proposal.remove_fields)
172	    for f in sorted(touched):
173	        if f in IMMUTABLE_FIELDS:
174	            rcs.append(RC_REPAIR_TOUCHED_IMMUTABLE)
175	        elif f in DERIVED_FIELDS:
176	            rcs.append(RC_REPAIR_TOUCHED_DERIVED)
177	        elif f not in TOP_LEVEL_FIELDS:
178	            rcs.append(RC_REPAIR_UNKNOWN_FIELD)
179	    return sorted(set(rcs))
180	
181	
182	def _act_of(card: dict):
183	    return EM.REGISTRY.acts.get(card.get('act') or '')
184	
185	
186	def _recompute_caches(card: dict, graph: P.Graph, policy: P.SourcePolicy) -> None:
187	    """Recompute every derived cache IN PLACE, exactly as Tier-0 expects it (so a repaired card passes
188	    DIM_CACHES): claim via `derive_claim`, span_numbers/has_number from `card['span']`, complete_tuple from
189	    `is_complete` and the act, and the source counts from the INDEPENDENTLY-VERIFIED evidence units — not
190	    the stored counts (Sol §Structure: n_sources equal verified units)."""
191	    act = _act_of(card)
192	    if act is not None:
193	        card['claim'] = EM.derive_claim(card, act)
194	        card['complete_tuple'] = bool(EM.is_complete(card) and act.tuple_bearing)
195	    card['span_numbers'] = sorted(EM.number_tokens(card.get('span') or ''))
196	    card['has_number'] = bool(card['span_numbers'])
197	    n = len(tier0._verified_evidence_units(card, graph, policy))
198	    if card.get('n_sources') is not None:
199	        card['n_sources'] = n
200	    if card.get('n_evidence_units') is not None:
201	        card['n_evidence_units'] = n

204	# =================================================================================================
205	# Edge classification — which corroborators are valid, and which independently entail the primary
206	# =================================================================================================
207	@dataclass
208	class EdgeStatus:
209	    index: int
210	    structurally_valid: bool          # a complete, independently-verifying binding (Tier-0)
211	    independently_entails: bool       # its own span entails the PRIMARY claim (report-AST)
212	    reason_codes: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
213	    detail: str = ''
214	
215	
216	def classify_edges(card: dict, graph: P.Graph, policy: P.SourcePolicy) -> list[EdgeStatus]:
217	    """For every `corroborating_sources` edge, decide (a) whether it has a complete independently-verifying
218	    binding (Tier-0 `screen_support_edge`) and (b) whether its OWN verified span independently entails the
219	    primary claim (report-AST, never inheriting the primary's pass — Sol §Faithfulness). Both are needed:
220	    a structurally valid edge that does not entail the primary is false corroboration."""
221	    edges = card.get('corroborating_sources') or []
222	    corr_faith = harness.audit_faithfulness_corroborators(card, graph)
223	    out: list[EdgeStatus] = []
224	    for i, edge in enumerate(edges):
225	        det = tier0.screen_support_edge(edge, graph, policy)
226	        struct_ok = det.verdict == PASS
227	        faith = corr_faith[i] if i < len(corr_faith) else None
228	        entails = bool(faith and faith.verdict == PASS)
229	        rcs = list(det.reason_codes)
230	        if faith and faith.verdict == FAIL:
231	            rcs.extend(faith.reason_codes)
232	        out.append(EdgeStatus(i, struct_ok, entails, sorted(set(rcs)),
233	                              det.detail or (faith.detail if faith else '')))
234	    return out
235

253	@dataclass
254	class CardDisposition:
255	    """The final, accounted outcome for ONE input top-level card. Exactly one `bucket`; the resulting
256	    object (a clean/repaired card, or an owned suggestion, or nothing) is carried explicitly, and the
257	    lineage records the before/after content hashes and the changed fields (Sol §REPAIR_TIGHTEN 7-8)."""
258	    audit_row_id: str
259	    card_id: str
260	    disposition: str                  # the Sol §4 verb finally applied
261	    bucket: str                       # a TOP_LEVEL_BUCKETS member
262	    original_hash: str
263	    result_hash: str = ''
264	    result_card: dict | None = None   # goes to audited_cards.json (kept / repaired / rebased primary)
265	    owned_suggestion: dict | None = None   # goes to owned_suggestions.json (demoted)
266	    quarantine_reason: str = ''
267	    reason_codes: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
268	    changed_fields: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
269	    rerun_overall: str = ''           # the deterministic rerun verdict after a repair
270	    rerun_final: str = ''             # the full-ladder rerun verdict after a repair (when a runner is used)
271	    edge_dispositions: list[EdgeDisposition] = field(default_factory=list)
272	    detail: str = ''
273	
274	    def to_json(self) -> dict:
275	        return dict(
276	            audit_row_id=self.audit_row_id, card_id=self.card_id, disposition=self.disposition,
277	            bucket=self.bucket, original_hash=self.original_hash, result_hash=self.result_hash,
278	            has_result_card=self.result_card is not None,
279	            has_owned_suggestion=self.owned_suggestion is not None,
280	            quarantine_reason=self.quarantine_reason, reason_codes=sorted(set(self.reason_codes)),
281	            changed_fields=list(self.changed_fields), rerun_overall=self.rerun_overall,
282	            rerun_final=self.rerun_final,
283	            edge_dispositions=[e.to_json() for e in self.edge_dispositions], detail=self.detail)

286	# =================================================================================================
287	# Applying each repairing disposition — always to a NEW object, then rerun (Sol Phase 4 step 5/7)
288	# =================================================================================================
289	def apply_typed_repair(card: dict, proposal: RepairProposal, graph: P.Graph,
290	                       policy: P.SourcePolicy) -> tuple[dict | None, list[str]]:
291	    """Sol §REPAIR_TIGHTEN: apply the TYPED field changes to a NEW copy, leaving source bytes/hash/
292	    manifestation/offsets immutable, then RECOMPUTE the claim and every derived cache (never accepting
293	    model-written claim prose). Returns (new_card, []) or (None, reason_codes) if the proposal is illegal."""
294	    illegal = validate_repair_proposal(proposal)
295	    if illegal:
296	        return None, illegal
297	    if not proposal.field_changes and not proposal.remove_fields:
298	        return None, [RC_REPAIR_NO_PROPOSAL]
299	    new = copy.deepcopy(card)
300	    for f in proposal.remove_fields:
301	        if f in new and isinstance(new.get(f), str):
302	            new[f] = ''            # remove a contaminated/unsupported OPTIONAL string field (Sol §REPAIR)
303	    for f, v in proposal.field_changes.items():
304	        new[f] = v
305	    _recompute_caches(new, graph, policy)
306	    return new, []
307	
308	
309	def apply_remove_bad_edges(card: dict, bad_indices: list[int], graph: P.Graph,
310	                           policy: P.SourcePolicy) -> tuple[dict, list[dict]]:
311	    """Sol §REMOVE_BAD_SUPPORT_EDGE: retain the primary, move each bad edge OUT (returned so it is
312	    quarantined WITH ITS REASON, never hidden), and recompute n_sources/n_evidence_units. The primary is a
313	    NEW superseding object because its counts changed."""
314	    new = copy.deepcopy(card)
315	    edges = list(new.get('corroborating_sources') or [])
316	    bad = set(bad_indices)
317	    removed = [edges[i] for i in sorted(bad) if 0 <= i < len(edges)]
318	    new['corroborating_sources'] = [e for i, e in enumerate(edges) if i not in bad]
319	    _recompute_caches(new, graph, policy)
320	    return new, removed
321	
322	
323	def _collision_safe_id(base_id: str, edge: dict, existing_ids: frozenset[str]) -> str | None:
324	    """Sol §REBASE: issue a COLLISION-SAFE card id for the promoted corroborator. Deterministic and
325	    address-based (manifestation + offsets), with a short content salt so two rebases from one span do not
326	    themselves collide. Returns None only if even the salted id is already taken."""
327	    mid = edge.get('manifestation_id') or ''
328	    s, e = edge.get('span_start'), edge.get('span_end')
329	    cand = f'{base_id}#rebase:{mid}:{s}-{e}'
330	    if cand not in existing_ids:
331	        return cand
332	    salt = hashlib.sha256(f'{cand}:{edge.get("span_raw", "")}'.encode('utf-8')).hexdigest()[:12]
333	    cand = f'{cand}:{salt}'
334	    return cand if cand not in existing_ids else None
335	
336	
337	def apply_rebase(card: dict, edge_index: int, graph: P.Graph, policy: P.SourcePolicy,
338	                 existing_ids: frozenset[str]) -> tuple[dict | None, list[str]]:
339	    """Sol §REBASE_TO_VALID_SUPPORT: the primary failed, but corroborator `edge_index` INDEPENDENTLY
340	    entails the claim. Rebuild a COMPLETE binding from that source's graph bytes and make it the primary:
341	    move the binding coordinates (manifestation/hash/offsets/span/permitted set), recompute attribution and
342	    counts from the LIVE graph (never a copied attribution string), issue a collision-safe id, and drop the
343	    promoted edge from the corroborator list. The claim's typed fields are unchanged, so `derive_claim`
344	    yields the same claim — now bound to bytes that actually support it. Returns (new_card, []) or
345	    (None, reason_codes)."""
346	    edges = list(card.get('corroborating_sources') or [])
347	    if not (0 <= edge_index < len(edges)):
348	        return None, [RC_REBASE_NO_VALID_SUPPORT]
349	    edge = edges[edge_index]
350	    binding = RA._binding_from_card(edge)
351	    if not binding or not graph.verify_span(binding):
352	        return None, [RC_REBASE_NO_VALID_SUPPORT]
353	    try:
354	        att = graph.resolve_attribution(binding, policy)
355	    except KeyError:
356	        return None, [RC_REBASE_NO_VALID_SUPPORT]
357	    if not att.admitted:
358	        return None, [RC_REBASE_NO_VALID_SUPPORT]
359	    m = graph.manifestations.get(binding['manifestation_id'])
360	    if m is None:
361	        return None, [RC_REBASE_NO_VALID_SUPPORT]
362	
363	    new = copy.deepcopy(card)
364	    new_id = _collision_safe_id(card.get('id') or 'card', edge, existing_ids)
365	    if new_id is None:
366	        return None, [RC_REBASE_ID_COLLISION]
367	    new['id'] = new_id
368	    # move the binding coordinates onto the primary
369	    new['manifestation_id'] = binding['manifestation_id']
370	    new['content_hash'] = binding['content_hash']
371	    new['span_start'] = binding['span_start']
372	    new['span_end'] = binding['span_end']
373	    new['span_raw'] = binding['text']
374	    new['span'] = edge.get('span') or binding['text']
375	    new['permitted_expression_ids'] = list(binding.get('permitted_expression_ids') or [])
376	    # recompute identity + attribution from the LIVE graph (never copy the attribution string)
377	    new['expression_id'] = m.expression_id
378	    new['work_id'] = m.work_id
379	    new['evidence_unit_id'] = m.work_id
380	    new['attribution_target_expression_id'] = att.names_expression_id
381	    if 'attribution' in new:
382	        new['attribution'] = att.text or ''
383	    w = graph.works.get(m.work_id)
384	    if w is not None:
385	        for k, wv in (('authors', w.authors), ('venue', w.venue), ('year', w.year), ('doi', w.doi)):
386	            if k in new:
387	                new[k] = wv
388	    new['source_version'] = m.content_hash[:12]
389	    # the promoted edge leaves the corroborator list
390	    new['corroborating_sources'] = [e for i, e in enumerate(edges) if i != edge_index]
391	    _recompute_caches(new, graph, policy)
392	    return new, []
393	
394	
395	# =================================================================================================
396	# OWNED demotion — the gate IS report_ast.validate_node(Owned(...)) (Sol §DEMOTE, §1)
397	# =================================================================================================
398	def validate_owned_demotion(text: str, premise_ids, bundle: RA.CardBundle) -> list[RA.Failure]:
399	    """Sol §DEMOTE_TO_OWNED_SUGGESTION: a demotion is sound ONLY when the reviewer-voice replacement passes
400	    `report_ast.validate_node(Owned(text, premise_ids), bundle)`. That one call already enforces every one
401	    of Sol's demotion constraints — it refuses a number, a named source, an oblique attribution to an
402	    actor, a bare first-order finding, a novel particular, and a synthesis naming fewer than two resolvable
403	    premises. Returns the (possibly empty) list of failures; empty means the demotion is admissible."""
404	    owned = RA.Owned(text=text, premise_ids=tuple(premise_ids or ()))
405	    return RA.validate_node(0, owned, bundle)
406	
407	
408	def build_owned_suggestion(card: dict, text: str, premise_ids) -> dict:
409	    """The record that moves to owned_suggestions.json (Sol §DEMOTE: it does NOT remain in audited_cards).
410	    It is NOT citeable evidence — it carries the reviewer-voice text, the passing premise ids it is a
411	    synthesis over (if any), and a back-pointer to the card it was demoted from, and NOTHING that would let
412	    it be resolved as a bound claim."""
413	    return dict(kind='owned_suggestion', text=text, premise_ids=list(premise_ids or ()),
414	                demoted_from_card_id=card.get('id') or '')
415

417	# =================================================================================================
418	# The orchestrator — turn one proposed disposition into one accounted outcome, RERUNNING every repair
419	# =================================================================================================
420	def _rerun(card: dict, graph: P.Graph, policy: P.SourcePolicy, *, taxonomy, tagger, runner,
421	           question: str, contract_facets: list[str]) -> tuple[DeterministicReceipt, object]:
422	    """Sol Phase 4 step 7 / task: RE-RUN the entire audit stack on a repaired card. The deterministic
423	    screen always reruns; the full semantic ladder reruns too when an Opus transport is injected. Returns
424	    (deterministic_receipt, combined_verdict_or_None)."""
425	    det = tier0.screen_card(card, graph, policy, taxonomy=taxonomy, tagger=tagger, json_pointer='/rerun')
426	    combined = None
427	    if runner is not None:
428	        combined = harness.audit_card(card, graph, det, question=question,
429	                                      contract_facets=contract_facets, runner=runner)
430	    return det, combined
431	
432	
433	def _repair_holds(det: DeterministicReceipt, combined, card: dict, graph: P.Graph) -> tuple[bool, str]:
434	    """A repair is ACCEPTED only if the rerun clears every gate that can be checked (Sol §REPAIR 4-6):
435	    the deterministic screen must not FAIL, report-AST faithfulness must not FAIL, and — when an Opus
436	    transport ran — the full-ladder verdict must be PASS. Otherwise the repair did NOT hold and the card is
437	    quarantined (never silently kept)."""
438	    if det.overall == FAIL:
439	        return False, 'deterministic rerun still fails'
440	    faith = harness.audit_faithfulness_primary(card, graph)
441	    if faith.verdict == FAIL:
442	        return False, 'report-AST faithfulness rerun still fails'
443	    if combined is not None and combined.final != PASS:
444	        return False, f'full-ladder rerun did not pass ({combined.final})'
445	    return True, ''
446	
447	
448	def dispose_card(card: dict, combined, det_receipt: DeterministicReceipt, *,
449	                 graph: P.Graph, policy: P.SourcePolicy, taxonomy=None, tagger=None,
450	                 runner=None, question: str = '', contract_facets: list[str] | None = None,
451	                 proposal: RepairProposal | None = None,
452	                 existing_ids: frozenset[str] = frozenset(),
453	                 bundle: RA.CardBundle | None = None) -> CardDisposition:
454	    """Turn ONE card's `CombinedVerdict` (with its proposed disposition) into exactly one accounted
455	    `CardDisposition`. Every repairing disposition rebuilds the card as a NEW object and RE-RUNS the whole
456	    stack; a repair that does not hold falls closed to QUARANTINE. Support edges are classified and
457	    accounted separately. Nothing is silently dropped."""
458	    contract_facets = contract_facets or []
459	    rid = det_receipt.audit_row_id
460	    cid = card.get('id') or ''
461	    original_hash = card_content_hash(card)
462	    proposed = combined.proposed_disposition
463	
464	    def _quarantine(reason_codes, detail, edges=None):
465	        return CardDisposition(rid, cid, QUARANTINE_CARD, BUCKET_QUARANTINED, original_hash,
466	                               quarantine_reason=detail, reason_codes=sorted(set(reason_codes or [RC_QUARANTINE])),
467	                               detail=detail, edge_dispositions=edges or _edge_dispositions_all_quarantined(card))
468	
469	    # --- KEEP_UNCHANGED --------------------------------------------------------------------------
470	    if proposed == KEEP_UNCHANGED and combined.final == PASS:
471	        edges = _edge_dispositions_from_status(classify_edges(card, graph, policy))
472	        return CardDisposition(rid, cid, KEEP_UNCHANGED, BUCKET_KEPT, original_hash,
473	                               result_hash=original_hash, result_card=copy.deepcopy(card),
474	                               reason_codes=list(combined.reason_codes), edge_dispositions=edges,
475	                               detail='all applicable dimensions pass')
476	
477	    # --- QUARANTINE_CARD (explicit) --------------------------------------------------------------
478	    if proposed == QUARANTINE_CARD:
479	        return _quarantine(combined.reason_codes, combined.detail or 'quarantine')
480	
481	    # --- REMOVE_BAD_SUPPORT_EDGE -----------------------------------------------------------------
482	    if proposed == REMOVE_BAD_SUPPORT_EDGE:
483	        statuses = classify_edges(card, graph, policy)
484	        bad = [s.index for s in statuses if not (s.structurally_valid and s.independently_entails)]
485	        if not bad:
486	            # nothing actually wrong with the edges — treat as keep if the card itself passes
487	            if combined.final == PASS:
488	                edges = _edge_dispositions_from_status(statuses)
489	                return CardDisposition(rid, cid, KEEP_UNCHANGED, BUCKET_KEPT, original_hash,
490	                                       result_hash=original_hash, result_card=copy.deepcopy(card),
491	                                       edge_dispositions=edges, detail='no bad edge to remove')
492	            return _quarantine([RC_REMOVE_PRIMARY_NOT_VALID], 'primary not valid and no bad edge to remove')
493	        new, removed = apply_remove_bad_edges(card, bad, graph, policy)
494	        det, comb = _rerun(new, graph, policy, taxonomy=taxonomy, tagger=tagger, runner=runner,
495	                           question=question, contract_facets=contract_facets)
496	        held, why = _repair_holds(det, comb, new, graph)
497	        edge_disp = []
498	        for s in statuses:
499	            if s.index in bad:
500	                edge_disp.append(EdgeDisposition(s.index, EDGE_QUARANTINED, s.reason_codes,
501	                                                 s.detail or 'false or unverifiable corroboration'))
502	            else:
503	                edge_disp.append(EdgeDisposition(s.index, EDGE_KEPT, s.reason_codes))
504	        if not held:
505	            return _quarantine([RC_REPAIR_RERUN_FAILED], f'remove-edge repair did not hold: {why}', edge_disp)
506	        return CardDisposition(rid, cid, REMOVE_BAD_SUPPORT_EDGE, BUCKET_REPAIRED, original_hash,
507	                               result_hash=card_content_hash(new), result_card=new,
508	                               reason_codes=[RC_QUARANTINE_EDGE], changed_fields=_changed_fields(card, new),
509	                               rerun_overall=det.overall, rerun_final=(comb.final if comb else ''),
510	                               edge_dispositions=edge_disp,
511	                               detail=f'removed {len(removed)} bad edge(s); counts recomputed')
512	
513	    # --- REBASE_TO_VALID_SUPPORT -----------------------------------------------------------------
514	    if proposed == REBASE_TO_VALID_SUPPORT:
515	        statuses = classify_edges(card, graph, policy)
516	        idx = proposal.rebase_edge_index if (proposal and proposal.rebase_edge_index is not None) else None
517	        if idx is None:
518	            supporting = [s.index for s in statuses if s.structurally_valid and s.independently_entails]
519	            idx = supporting[0] if supporting else None
520	        if idx is None:
521	            return _quarantine([RC_REBASE_NO_VALID_SUPPORT],
522	                               'no corroborator independently supports the claim — cannot rebase')
523	        new, rcs = apply_rebase(card, idx, graph, policy, existing_ids)
524	        if new is None:
525	            return _quarantine(rcs, 'rebase could not rebuild a valid primary binding')
526	        det, comb = _rerun(new, graph, policy, taxonomy=taxonomy, tagger=tagger, runner=runner,
527	                           question=question, contract_facets=contract_facets)
528	        held, why = _repair_holds(det, comb, new, graph)
529	        edge_disp = [EdgeDisposition(idx, EDGE_REPAIRED, [], 'promoted to primary')]
530	        edge_disp += _edge_dispositions_from_status([s for s in statuses if s.index != idx])
531	        if not held:
532	            return _quarantine([RC_REPAIR_RERUN_FAILED], f'rebase did not hold: {why}', edge_disp)
533	        return CardDisposition(rid, cid, REBASE_TO_VALID_SUPPORT, BUCKET_REPAIRED, original_hash,
534	                               result_hash=card_content_hash(new), result_card=new,
535	                               changed_fields=_changed_fields(card, new), rerun_overall=det.overall,
536	                               rerun_final=(comb.final if comb else ''), edge_dispositions=edge_disp,
537	                               detail=f'rebased onto corroborator[{idx}] with a fresh collision-safe id')
538	
539	    # --- REPAIR_TIGHTEN --------------------------------------------------------------------------
540	    if proposed == REPAIR_TIGHTEN:
541	        if proposal is None:
542	            return _quarantine([RC_REPAIR_NO_PROPOSAL],
543	                               'repair proposed but no typed field-change proposal supplied — fail closed')
544	        new, rcs = apply_typed_repair(card, proposal, graph, policy)
545	        if new is None:
546	            return _quarantine(rcs, 'repair proposal was illegal (immutable/derived/unknown field)')
547	        det, comb = _rerun(new, graph, policy, taxonomy=taxonomy, tagger=tagger, runner=runner,
548	                           question=question, contract_facets=contract_facets)
549	        held, why = _repair_holds(det, comb, new, graph)
550	        if not held:
551	            return _quarantine([RC_REPAIR_RERUN_FAILED], f'tighten did not hold: {why}')
552	        edges = _edge_dispositions_from_status(classify_edges(new, graph, policy))
553	        return CardDisposition(rid, cid, REPAIR_TIGHTEN, BUCKET_REPAIRED, original_hash,
554	                               result_hash=card_content_hash(new), result_card=new,
555	                               changed_fields=_changed_fields(card, new), rerun_overall=det.overall,
556	                               rerun_final=(comb.final if comb else ''), edge_dispositions=edges,
557	                               detail='typed tighten; claim recomputed via derive_claim')
558	
559	    # --- DEMOTE_TO_OWNED_SUGGESTION --------------------------------------------------------------
560	    if proposed == DEMOTE_TO_OWNED_SUGGESTION:
561	        # Sol §Voice: block the one illegal ATTRIBUTED->OWNED laundering transition (an unreachable card
562	        # has no source bytes to demote FROM).
563	        faith = harness.audit_faithfulness_primary(card, graph)
564	        if harness.voice_launder_blocked(DEMOTE_TO_OWNED_SUGGESTION, faith):
565	            return _quarantine([RC_DEMOTE_VOICE_LAUNDER],
566	                               'OWNED demotion of an unreachable card blocked — no source bytes to demote')
567	        if proposal is None or not (proposal.owned_text or '').strip():
568	            return _quarantine([RC_DEMOTE_OWNED_REJECTED],
569	                               'demotion proposed but no reviewer-voice OWNED replacement supplied')
570	        if bundle is None:
571	            return _quarantine([RC_DEMOTE_OWNED_REJECTED],
572	                               'no clean CardBundle to validate the OWNED replacement against')
573	        failures = validate_owned_demotion(proposal.owned_text, proposal.owned_premise_ids, bundle)
574	        if failures:
575	            return _quarantine([RC_DEMOTE_OWNED_REJECTED],
576	                               f'OWNED replacement rejected: {failures[0].reason}')
577	        suggestion = build_owned_suggestion(card, proposal.owned_text, proposal.owned_premise_ids)
578	        return CardDisposition(rid, cid, DEMOTE_TO_OWNED_SUGGESTION, BUCKET_DEMOTED, original_hash,
579	                               owned_suggestion=suggestion, detail='validated OWNED suggestion; not citeable',
580	                               edge_dispositions=_edge_dispositions_all_quarantined(card))
581	
582	    # --- Anything else (UNCERTAIN with an unhandled disposition) fails CLOSED ---------------------
583	    return _quarantine(combined.reason_codes or [RC_QUARANTINE],
584	                       f'unresolved/unsupported disposition {proposed!r} — fail closed')
585	
586	
587	def _edge_dispositions_from_status(statuses: list[EdgeStatus]) -> list[EdgeDisposition]:
588	    """A passing primary keeps every VALID corroborator and quarantines any false/unverifiable one."""
589	    out: list[EdgeDisposition] = []
590	    for s in statuses:
591	        if s.structurally_valid and s.independently_entails:
592	            out.append(EdgeDisposition(s.index, EDGE_KEPT, s.reason_codes))
593	        else:
594	            out.append(EdgeDisposition(s.index, EDGE_QUARANTINED, s.reason_codes,
595	                                       s.detail or 'false or unverifiable corroboration'))
596	    return out
597	
598	
599	def _edge_dispositions_all_quarantined(card: dict) -> list[EdgeDisposition]:
600	    """When the primary itself is quarantined or demoted, its support edges cannot ship either; they are
601	    quarantined WITH the card, never hidden (Sol: nothing silently dropped)."""
602	    return [EdgeDisposition(i, EDGE_QUARANTINED, [RC_QUARANTINE_EDGE], 'primary card not in clean set')
603	            for i in range(len(card.get('corroborating_sources') or []))]
604	
605	
606	# =================================================================================================
607	# Corpus reconciliation — the accounting invariant (Sol Phase 4 acceptance): nothing unaccounted
608	# =================================================================================================
609	class AccountingError(Exception):
610	    """The census did not reconcile: a row or edge left the engine unaccounted (Sol Phase 4: no
611	    unaccounted row or edge is allowed)."""
612	
613	
614	def reconcile_corpus(dispositions: list[CardDisposition], input_cards: list[dict]) -> dict:
615	    """Sol Phase 4 acceptance. Assert:
616	         input_top_level    = kept_unchanged + repaired_and_superseded + quarantined + demoted
617	         input_support_edges = kept_support_edges + repaired_support_edges + quarantined_support_edges
618	    Every input row and every support edge must appear in exactly one bucket. Raises AccountingError on any
619	    mismatch; otherwise returns the reconciled census."""
620	    n_input = len(input_cards)
621	    n_input_edges = sum(len(c.get('corroborating_sources') or []) for c in input_cards)
622	
623	    top = {b: 0 for b in TOP_LEVEL_BUCKETS}
624	    edge = {b: 0 for b in EDGE_BUCKETS}
625	    seen_rids: set[str] = set()
626	    for d in dispositions:
627	        if d.bucket not in TOP_LEVEL_BUCKETS:
628	            raise AccountingError(f'card {d.card_id!r} has unknown bucket {d.bucket!r}')
629	        if d.audit_row_id in seen_rids:
630	            raise AccountingError(f'audit_row_id {d.audit_row_id!r} accounted twice')
631	        seen_rids.add(d.audit_row_id)
632	        top[d.bucket] += 1
633	        for e in d.edge_dispositions:
634	            if e.bucket not in EDGE_BUCKETS:
635	                raise AccountingError(f'edge {d.card_id}[{e.index}] has unknown bucket {e.bucket!r}')
636	            edge[e.bucket] += 1
637	
638	    if len(dispositions) != n_input:
639	        raise AccountingError(f'{len(dispositions)} dispositions for {n_input} input cards')
640	    top_sum = sum(top.values())
641	    if top_sum != n_input:
642	        raise AccountingError(f'top-level buckets sum to {top_sum}, expected {n_input}')
643	    edge_sum = sum(edge.values())
644	    if edge_sum != n_input_edges:
645	        raise AccountingError(f'support-edge buckets sum to {edge_sum}, expected {n_input_edges}')
646	
647	    return dict(
648	        input_top_level=n_input, input_support_edges=n_input_edges,
649	        top_level=top, support_edges=edge,
650	        reconciled=(top_sum == n_input and edge_sum == n_input_edges),
651	    )
652

content.text: Now let me look at the existing tests to understand fixture conventions.

content.content:
1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""Acceptance for the Tier-0 deterministic evidence-card screen (Sol §3 Tier 0, Phase 1, Phase 8).
3	
4	Hermetic: it builds the repo's synthetic bound-card graph (`scripts/_test_fixtures.build`), constructs
5	a fully v2-shaped card that is deterministically clean, then plants ONE fault at a time and asserts the
6	INTENDED Tier-0 dimension — and only a determinate outcome — catches it. This is the "removing or
7	disabling any critical check makes at least one adversary test fail" law, at Tier-0 scope.
8	
9	No task-72 / DOI / subject literal appears here beyond the neutral synthetic fixture. Every assertion is
10	about STRUCTURE.
11	"""
12	from __future__ import annotations
13	
14	import copy
15	import hashlib
16	import sys
17	from pathlib import Path
18	
19	import pytest
20	
21	ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
22	sys.path.insert(0, str(ROOT / 'scripts'))
23	sys.path.insert(0, str(ROOT / 'src'))
24	
25	import provenance as P                       # noqa: E402
26	import evidence_miner as EM                  # noqa: E402
27	import _test_fixtures as TF                  # noqa: E402
28	from card_audit import tier0                 # noqa: E402
29	from card_audit.audit_schema import (        # noqa: E402
30	    PASS, FAIL, NOT_APPLICABLE, NEEDS_OPUS, NEEDS_CONTRACT,
31	    DIM_STRUCTURE, DIM_BINDING, DIM_CACHES, DIM_NUMERIC, DIM_COT, DIM_FACET, DIM_CORROBORATOR,
32	    RC_SCHEMA_UNKNOWN_FIELD, RC_SCHEMA_DUP_ID, RC_BINDING_SPAN_UNVERIFIED, RC_CACHE_CLAIM_MISMATCH,
33	    RC_CACHE_COUNTS, RC_NUMERIC_FABRICATED, RC_NUMERIC_UNIT, RC_COT_SCAFFOLD, RC_FACET_NOT_IN_TAXONOMY,
34	    RC_CORR_INCOMPLETE_BINDING, DERIVED_CACHE, ATOMIC_EVIDENCE_VALUE, SOURCE_BYTES, EMPTY,
35	    CONTENT_CLASSES,
36	)
37	
38	POLICY = P.JOURNAL_ONLY
39	TAXONOMY = frozenset({'automation', 'labor'})
40	
41	
42	def _tagger(_span: str) -> list[str]:
43	    return ['automation']
44	
45	
46	def _base_fields():
47	    """The full closed v2 surface, every optional field present-but-empty so only what we set is live."""
48	    return dict(
49	        claim='', span='', span_raw='', span_start=-1, span_end=-1, span_numbers=[], has_number=False,
50	        level='', horizon='', method='', mechanisms=[], doi='', authors=[], venue='', year='',
51	        attribution='', source='', work_id='', evidence_unit_id='', expression_id='',
52	        attribution_target_expression_id='', permitted_expression_ids=[], manifestation_id='',
53	        content_hash='', source_policy='', act='', act_registry_version=EM.REGISTRY.version,
54	        card_kind='qualitative', effect='', unit='', comparator='', outcome='', finding='', holding='',
55	        authority='', recommendation='', limitation='', population='', geography='', period='',
56	        technology='', industry='', unit_of_analysis='', design='', uncertainty='', study_design='',
57	        geographic_scope='', section='results', section_weight=0.5, context_start=0, context_end=10,
58	        field_provenance={}, source_version='', text_field='fulltext', facet_tags=['automation'],
59	        facet_tags_span=['automation'], complete_tuple=False, corroborating_sources=[],
60	        same_unit_other_expressions=[], n_sources=1, n_evidence_units=1,
61	    )
62	
63	
64	@pytest.fixture(scope='module')
65	def graph_and_clean():
66	    g, _ = TF.build()
67	    # Stamp a positive identity verdict on the synthetic bytes so resolve_attribution admits them —
68	    # in production the miner writes this profile field; the fixture graph does not. SAME_WORK is an
69	    # IDENTITY_PROVEN member. (This is test scaffolding only; the screen reads the verdict, never sets it.)
70	    _kind_verdict = {'journal_version': 'VERSION_OF_PUBLISHED', 'working_paper': 'VERSION_OF_PREPRINT'}
71	    for m in g.manifestations.values():
72	        kind = g.expressions[m.expression_id].kind
73	        m.profile['semantic_binding'] = _kind_verdict.get(kind, 'SAME_WORK')
74	    mid = 'm:bres'
75	    m = g.manifestations[mid]
76	    span = TF.BRES_SPAN
77	    s = m.text.index(span)
78	    b = g.bind_span(mid, s, s + len(span))
79	    att = g.resolve_attribution(b, POLICY)
80	    assert att.admitted, att.refusal
81	    w = g.works[m.work_id]
82	    # a finding that is a verbatim slice of the span (so the CoT test can prove it clean offline)
83	    finding = 'Computer automation of such work has been correspondingly limited in its scope'
84	    card = _base_fields()
85	    card.update(
86	        id='c:clean', manifestation_id=mid, content_hash=b['content_hash'],
87	        span_start=s, span_end=s + len(span), span_raw=b['text'], span=span,
88	        expression_id=b['expression_id'], permitted_expression_ids=list(b['permitted_expression_ids']),
89	        attribution_target_expression_id=att.names_expression_id,
90	        attribution=att.text or '', work_id=m.work_id, evidence_unit_id=m.work_id,
91	        authors=w.authors, venue=w.venue, year=w.year, doi=w.doi, source_version=m.content_hash[:12],
92	        source_policy=POLICY.name, act='qualitative_empirical_result', finding=finding,
93	    )
94	    act = EM.REGISTRY.acts['qualitative_empirical_result']
95	    card['claim'] = EM.derive_claim(card, act)
96	    card['span_numbers'] = sorted(EM.number_tokens(span))
97	    card['has_number'] = bool(card['span_numbers'])
98	    return g, card
99	
100	
101	def _screen(card, g):
102	    return tier0.screen_card(card, g, POLICY, taxonomy=TAXONOMY, tagger=_tagger, json_pointer='/0')
103	
104	
105	def test_clean_card_passes_every_deterministic_dimension(graph_and_clean):
106	    g, card = graph_and_clean
107	    r = _screen(card, g)
108	    for name, d in r.dimensions.items():
109	        assert d.verdict in (PASS, NOT_APPLICABLE), f'{name} -> {d.verdict} :: {d.detail}'
110	    assert r.overall == PASS, [d.detail for d in r.dimensions.values() if d.verdict == FAIL]
111	    # every non-empty field got a legal content class, none unclassified
112	    for c in r.content_classes:
113	        assert c.content_class in (CONTENT_CLASSES | {''})
114	        assert c.verdict == PASS, f'{c.field} -> {c.verdict} ({c.reason_code})'
115	    # the display cache recomputes; the finding is proven source bytes
116	    classes = {c.field: c for c in r.content_classes}
117	    assert classes['claim'].content_class == DERIVED_CACHE
118	    assert classes['finding'].content_class == ATOMIC_EVIDENCE_VALUE
119	    assert classes['span_raw'].content_class == SOURCE_BYTES
120	
121	
122	def test_receipt_is_deterministic(graph_and_clean):
123	    g, card = graph_and_clean
124	    a, b = _screen(card, g).to_json(), _screen(card, g).to_json()
125	    assert a == b
126	    # renaming the id changes only the row id and card_id, never a verdict (metamorphic)
127	    renamed = copy.deepcopy(card)
128	    renamed['id'] = 'c:clean-renamed'
129	    r0, r1 = _screen(card, g), _screen(renamed, g)
130	    assert {k: v.verdict for k, v in r0.dimensions.items()} == \
131	           {k: v.verdict for k, v in r1.dimensions.items()}
132	
133	
134	# ---- planted single-fault adversaries: each caught by its INTENDED dimension --------------------
135	
136	def test_unknown_field_fails_structure(graph_and_clean):
137	    g, card = graph_and_clean
138	    bad = copy.deepcopy(card)
139	    bad['reasoning_trace'] = 'let me think about which number to pick'
140	    r = _screen(bad, g)
141	    assert r.dimensions[DIM_STRUCTURE].verdict == FAIL
142	    assert RC_SCHEMA_UNKNOWN_FIELD in r.dimensions[DIM_STRUCTURE].reason_codes
143	    assert r.overall == FAIL
144	
145	
146	def test_tampered_offsets_fail_binding(graph_and_clean):
147	    g, card = graph_and_clean
148	    bad = copy.deepcopy(card)
149	    bad['span_end'] = bad['span_start']          # empty/reversed window: text[s:s] == ''
150	    r = _screen(bad, g)
151	    assert r.dimensions[DIM_BINDING].verdict == FAIL
152	    assert RC_BINDING_SPAN_UNVERIFIED in r.dimensions[DIM_BINDING].reason_codes
153	
154	
155	def test_wrong_content_hash_fails_binding(graph_and_clean):
156	    g, card = graph_and_clean
157	    bad = copy.deepcopy(card)
158	    bad['content_hash'] = hashlib.sha256(b'not the bytes').hexdigest()
159	    assert _screen(bad, g).dimensions[DIM_BINDING].verdict == FAIL
160	
161	
162	def test_claim_not_derive_claim_fails_caches(graph_and_clean):
163	    g, card = graph_and_clean
164	    bad = copy.deepcopy(card)
165	    bad['claim'] = 'Automation doubled worldwide, causing mass unemployment.'
166	    r = _screen(bad, g)
167	    assert r.dimensions[DIM_CACHES].verdict == FAIL
168	    assert RC_CACHE_CLAIM_MISMATCH in r.dimensions[DIM_CACHES].reason_codes
169	
170	
171	def test_fabricated_number_fails_numeric(graph_and_clean):
172	    """A claim that asserts a figure the span never states (Sol §Numeric): caught mechanically, offline.
173	    We build a numeric card on the LEAK span (span says 10.25) and claim a fabricated 0.2."""
174	    g, _ = graph_and_clean
175	    mid = 'm:leak'
176	    m = g.manifestations[mid]
177	    span = TF.LEAK_SPAN
178	    s = m.text.index(span)
179	    b = g.bind_span(mid, s, s + len(span))
180	    att = g.resolve_attribution(b, POLICY)
181	    w = g.works[m.work_id]
182	    card = _base_fields()
183	    card.update(
184	        id='c:num', manifestation_id=mid, content_hash=b['content_hash'], span_start=s,
185	        span_end=s + len(span), span_raw=b['text'], span=span, expression_id=b['expression_id'],
186	        permitted_expression_ids=list(b['permitted_expression_ids']),
187	        attribution_target_expression_id=att.names_expression_id, attribution=att.text or '',
188	        work_id=m.work_id, evidence_unit_id=m.work_id, authors=w.authors, venue=w.venue, year=w.year,
189	        doi=w.doi, source_version=m.content_hash[:12], source_policy=POLICY.name,
190	        act='qualitative_empirical_result', finding='productivity growth of 10.25 percent was observed',
191	    )
192	    card['span_numbers'] = sorted(EM.number_tokens(span))
193	    card['has_number'] = True
194	    act = EM.REGISTRY.acts['qualitative_empirical_result']
195	    card['claim'] = EM.derive_claim(card, act)
196	    # honest numeric card first: numeric dimension must not fire
197	    assert tier0.screen_card(card, g, POLICY, taxonomy=TAXONOMY, tagger=_tagger,
198	                             json_pointer='/0').dimensions[DIM_NUMERIC].verdict in (PASS, NOT_APPLICABLE)
199	    # now inject a fabricated number into the claim cache (bypassing derive) -> numeric FAIL
200	    bad = copy.deepcopy(card)
201	    bad['claim'] = 'productivity fell 0.2 percent'
202	    r = tier0.screen_card(bad, g, POLICY, taxonomy=TAXONOMY, tagger=_tagger, json_pointer='/0')
203	    assert r.dimensions[DIM_NUMERIC].verdict == FAIL
204	    assert RC_NUMERIC_FABRICATED in r.dimensions[DIM_NUMERIC].reason_codes
205	
206	
207	def test_percent_vs_pp_swap_fails_numeric(graph_and_clean):
208	    g, _ = graph_and_clean
209	    mid = 'm:ar'                                  # AR span: "0.2 percentage points"
210	    m = g.manifestations[mid]
211	    span = TF.AR_SPAN
212	    s = m.text.index(span)
213	    b = g.bind_span(mid, s, s + len(span))
214	    card = _base_fields()
215	    card.update(
216	        id='c:pp', manifestation_id=mid, content_hash=b['content_hash'], span_start=s,
217	        span_end=s + len(span), span_raw=b['text'], span=span, expression_id=b['expression_id'],
218	        permitted_expression_ids=list(b['permitted_expression_ids']),
219	        work_id=m.work_id, evidence_unit_id=m.work_id, source_policy=POLICY.name,
220	        act='qualitative_empirical_result', finding='employment falls',
221	        claim='employment falls 0.2 percent',    # span says percentage POINTS, claim says percent
222	        span_numbers=sorted(EM.number_tokens(span)), has_number=True,
223	    )
224	    r = tier0.screen_card(card, g, POLICY, taxonomy=TAXONOMY, tagger=_tagger, json_pointer='/0')
225	    assert r.dimensions[DIM_NUMERIC].verdict == FAIL
226	    assert RC_NUMERIC_UNIT in r.dimensions[DIM_NUMERIC].reason_codes
227	
228	
229	def test_cot_scaffold_field_fails_cot(graph_and_clean):
230	    g, card = graph_and_clean
231	    bad = copy.deepcopy(card)
232	    bad['finding'] = '{"reason": "the user wants a strong finding", "pick": "option 2"}'
233	    r = _screen(bad, g)
234	    assert r.dimensions[DIM_COT].verdict == FAIL
235	    assert RC_COT_SCAFFOLD in r.dimensions[DIM_COT].reason_codes
236	
237	
238	def test_offtopic_facet_not_in_taxonomy_fails_facet(graph_and_clean):
239	    g, card = graph_and_clean
240	    bad = copy.deepcopy(card)
241	    bad['facet_tags_span'] = ['oncology']        # not a member of the pinned taxonomy
242	    bad['facet_tags'] = ['oncology']
243	    r = tier0.screen_card(bad, g, POLICY, taxonomy=TAXONOMY, tagger=None, json_pointer='/0')
244	    assert r.dimensions[DIM_FACET].verdict == FAIL
245	    assert RC_FACET_NOT_IN_TAXONOMY in r.dimensions[DIM_FACET].reason_codes
246	
247	
248	def test_unpinned_taxonomy_never_silently_passes(graph_and_clean):
249	    g, card = graph_and_clean
250	    r = tier0.screen_card(card, g, POLICY, taxonomy=None, tagger=None, json_pointer='/0')
251	    assert r.dimensions[DIM_FACET].verdict == NEEDS_CONTRACT
252	    assert r.overall == NEEDS_OPUS               # never composer-ready without a pinned contract
253	
254	
255	def test_incomplete_corroborator_binding_fails_edge(graph_and_clean):
256	    """The live miner serializes corroborators WITHOUT span_raw / permitted_expression_ids — Sol's
257	    lossy-consolidation defect. A nested edge that cannot be independently verified must fail."""
258	    g, card = graph_and_clean
259	    bad = copy.deepcopy(card)
260	    bad['corroborating_sources'] = [dict(
261	        manifestation_id='m:autor', content_hash='x', span_start=1, span_end=2,
262	        evidence_unit_id='w:autor', span='some corroborating prose',   # no span_raw, no permitted ids
263	    )]
264	    bad['n_sources'] = 2
265	    bad['n_evidence_units'] = 2
266	    r = _screen(bad, g)
267	    assert r.dimensions[DIM_CORROBORATOR].verdict == FAIL
268	    assert RC_CORR_INCOMPLETE_BINDING in r.dimensions[DIM_CORROBORATOR].reason_codes
269	    # and the inflated count (2 stored, 1 verifiable) is caught independently
270	    assert r.dimensions[DIM_CACHES].verdict == FAIL
271	    assert RC_CACHE_COUNTS in r.dimensions[DIM_CACHES].reason_codes
272	
273	
274	def test_duplicate_id_is_caught_at_census(graph_and_clean):
275	    g, card = graph_and_clean
276	    twin = copy.deepcopy(card)
277	    receipts = tier0.screen_corpus([card, twin], g, POLICY, taxonomy=TAXONOMY, tagger=_tagger)
278	    assert all(RC_SCHEMA_DUP_ID in r.dimensions[DIM_STRUCTURE].reason_codes for r in receipts)
279	    # distinct audit_row_ids despite the identical id, so neither record can hide the other
280	    assert receipts[0].audit_row_id != receipts[1].audit_row_id
281	
282	
283	def test_compound_canary_caught_in_multiple_dimensions(graph_and_clean):
284	    """Sol Phase 8 compound canary: one card carrying several planted faults; every dimension runs even
285	    after the first failure, and the final overall is FAIL."""
286	    g, card = graph_and_clean
287	    bad = copy.deepcopy(card)
288	    bad['span_end'] = bad['span_start']                       # fabricated/empty binding
289	    bad['finding'] = '```json\n{"step": 1}\n```'             # CoT scaffold
290	    bad['claim'] = 'employment doubled 999 percent worldwide'  # fabricated number
291	    bad['facet_tags_span'] = ['oncology']                     # off-topic
292	    r = _screen(bad, g)
293	    assert r.dimensions[DIM_BINDING].verdict == FAIL
294	    assert r.dimensions[DIM_COT].verdict == FAIL
295	    assert r.dimensions[DIM_NUMERIC].verdict == FAIL
296	    assert r.dimensions[DIM_FACET].verdict == FAIL
297	    assert r.overall == FAIL
298

content.text: Let me look at the harness test to understand the OpusRunner stub conventions.

content.content:
1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""Acceptance for the Tier-1/2/3 Opus audit harness (Sol §3 Tier 1/2/3, §Faithfulness, §Numeric,
3	§Relevance, §Voice, §4, Phase 2/3/8).
4	
5	HERMETIC. There is NO real Opus call. The Opus transport is injected as a deterministic stub (exactly as
6	`report_ast.set_entailment_judge` stubs the faithfulness judge), so the whole tier ladder — schema
7	validation, model-metadata enforcement, fail-closed combination, adjudication floors — is exercised
8	offline while the real card mine is still running.
9	
10	Every assertion is about STRUCTURE. No task-72 / DOI / subject literal drives a verdict.
11	"""
12	from __future__ import annotations
13	
14	import copy
15	import json
16	import sys
17	from pathlib import Path
18	
19	import pytest
20	
21	ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
22	sys.path.insert(0, str(ROOT / 'scripts'))
23	sys.path.insert(0, str(ROOT / 'src'))
24	
25	import provenance as P                       # noqa: E402
26	import evidence_miner as EM                  # noqa: E402
27	import report_ast as RA                      # noqa: E402
28	import _test_fixtures as TF                  # noqa: E402
29	from card_audit import tier0, harness        # noqa: E402
30	from card_audit.audit_schema import PASS, FAIL, UNCERTAIN, NOT_APPLICABLE  # noqa: E402
31	from card_audit.harness import (             # noqa: E402
32	    NUMERIC_SUBDIMENSIONS, REL_DIRECT, REL_CONTEXT, PASS_A, PASS_B,
33	    FAITH_UNREACHABLE, OpusVerdict, OpusResponseInvalid, OpusUnavailable,
34	    RC_FAITH_NOT_ENTAILED, RC_FAITH_UNREACHABLE, RC_OPUS_MODEL, RC_OPUS_DISAGREE,
35	    RC_OPUS_ALLEGES_ATOM, RC_OPUS_RELEVANCE, RC_OPUS_TRANSPORT,
36	)
37	
38	POLICY = P.JOURNAL_ONLY
39	TAXONOMY = frozenset({'automation', 'labor'})
40	QUESTION = 'What is the measured effect under study?'
41	FACETS = ['automation', 'labor']
42	
43	
44	def _tagger(_span: str) -> list[str]:
45	    return ['automation']
46	
47	
48	def _base_fields():
49	    return dict(
50	        claim='', span='', span_raw='', span_start=-1, span_end=-1, span_numbers=[], has_number=False,
51	        level='', horizon='', method='', mechanisms=[], doi='', authors=[], venue='', year='',
52	        attribution='', source='', work_id='', evidence_unit_id='', expression_id='',
53	        attribution_target_expression_id='', permitted_expression_ids=[], manifestation_id='',
54	        content_hash='', source_policy='', act='', act_registry_version=EM.REGISTRY.version,
55	        card_kind='qualitative', effect='', unit='', comparator='', outcome='', finding='', holding='',
56	        authority='', recommendation='', limitation='', population='', geography='', period='',
57	        technology='', industry='', unit_of_analysis='', design='', uncertainty='', study_design='',
58	        geographic_scope='', section='results', section_weight=0.5, context_start=0, context_end=10,
59	        field_provenance={}, source_version='', text_field='fulltext', facet_tags=['automation'],
60	        facet_tags_span=['automation'], complete_tuple=False, corroborating_sources=[],
61	        same_unit_other_expressions=[], n_sources=1, n_evidence_units=1,
62	    )
63	
64	
65	@pytest.fixture(scope='module')
66	def graph_and_clean():
67	    g, _ = TF.build()
68	    _kv = {'journal_version': 'VERSION_OF_PUBLISHED', 'working_paper': 'VERSION_OF_PREPRINT'}
69	    for m in g.manifestations.values():
70	        m.profile['semantic_binding'] = _kv.get(g.expressions[m.expression_id].kind, 'SAME_WORK')
71	    mid = 'm:bres'
72	    m = g.manifestations[mid]
73	    span = TF.BRES_SPAN
74	    s = m.text.index(span)
75	    b = g.bind_span(mid, s, s + len(span))
76	    att = g.resolve_attribution(b, POLICY)
77	    assert att.admitted, att.refusal
78	    w = g.works[m.work_id]
79	    finding = 'Computer automation of such work has been correspondingly limited in its scope'
80	    card = _base_fields()
81	    card.update(
82	        id='c:clean', manifestation_id=mid, content_hash=b['content_hash'],
83	        span_start=s, span_end=s + len(span), span_raw=b['text'], span=span,
84	        expression_id=b['expression_id'], permitted_expression_ids=list(b['permitted_expression_ids']),
85	        attribution_target_expression_id=att.names_expression_id, attribution=att.text or '',
86	        work_id=m.work_id, evidence_unit_id=m.work_id, authors=w.authors, venue=w.venue, year=w.year,
87	        doi=w.doi, source_version=m.content_hash[:12], source_policy=POLICY.name,
88	        act='qualitative_empirical_result', finding=finding,
89	    )
90	    act = EM.REGISTRY.acts['qualitative_empirical_result']
91	    card['claim'] = EM.derive_claim(card, act)
92	    card['span_numbers'] = sorted(EM.number_tokens(span))
93	    card['has_number'] = bool(card['span_numbers'])
94	    return g, card
95	
96	
97	def _receipt(card, g):
98	    return tier0.screen_card(card, g, POLICY, taxonomy=TAXONOMY, tagger=_tagger, json_pointer='/0')
99	
100	
101	# -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
102	# Deterministic Opus transport stub — the injectable runner (prompt, schema) -> claude -p envelope
103	# -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
104	def _verdict(rid, *, faith=PASS, rel=(PASS, REL_DIRECT), numeric_applicable=False, numeric_fail=None,
105	            facet=PASS, cot_fail=False, disp='KEEP_UNCHANGED'):
106	    subs = {}
107	    if numeric_applicable:
108	        subs = {k: PASS for k in NUMERIC_SUBDIMENSIONS}
109	        if numeric_fail:
110	            subs[numeric_fail] = FAIL
111	    ccs = [{'field': 'finding', 'content_class': 'ATOMIC_EVIDENCE_VALUE',
112	            'verdict': FAIL if cot_fail else PASS}]
113	    return {
114	        'audit_row_id': rid,
115	        'faithfulness': {'verdict': faith},
116	        'numeric': {'applicable': numeric_applicable, 'subdimensions': subs},
117	        'relevance': {'verdict': rel[0], 'classification': rel[1]},
118	        'facet': {'verdict': facet},
119	        'content_classes': ccs,
120	        'proposed_disposition': disp,
121	    }
122	
123	
124	def _envelope(verdict, model='claude-opus-4-8'):
125	    return {'result': json.dumps(verdict), 'modelUsage': {model: {'inputTokens': 1}},
126	            'total_cost_usd': 0.01, 'is_error': False}
127	
128	
129	def _runner_for(verdict, model='claude-opus-4-8'):
130	    def run(_prompt, _schema):
131	        return _envelope(verdict, model)
132	    return run
133	
134	
135	# =================================================================================================
136	# Faithfulness reuses report_ast.entailed_by_span (Sol §1, §Faithfulness) — NOT reinvented
137	# =================================================================================================
138	def test_faithfulness_passes_when_span_entails_claim(graph_and_clean):
139	    g, card = graph_and_clean
140	    RA.set_entailment_judge(lambda clause, span: ('ENTAILED', 'ok'))
141	    try:
142	        r = harness.audit_faithfulness_primary(card, g)
143	    finally:
144	        RA.set_entailment_judge(None)
145	    assert r.verdict == PASS and r.faith_label == 'ENTAILED'
146	
147	
148	def test_faithfulness_fails_when_judge_rejects(graph_and_clean):
149	    g, card = graph_and_clean
150	    RA.set_entailment_judge(lambda clause, span: ('NOT_ENTAILED', 'nope'))
151	    try:
152	        r = harness.audit_faithfulness_primary(card, g)
153	    finally:
154	        RA.set_entailment_judge(None)
155	    assert r.verdict == FAIL and RC_FAITH_NOT_ENTAILED in r.reason_codes
156	
157	
158	def test_unreachable_span_is_never_faithful(graph_and_clean):
159	    """Sol §Faithfulness: a structurally unreachable span (binding does not verify) is UNREACHABLE, never
160	    PASS. Opus can never mark it faithful because the harness never even asks — it resolves to FAIL."""
161	    g, card = graph_and_clean
162	    bad = copy.deepcopy(card)
163	    bad['span_end'] = bad['span_start']                 # empty/reversed window -> verify_span fails
164	    RA.set_entailment_judge(lambda clause, span: ('ENTAILED', 'ok'))  # even a generous judge cannot save it
165	    try:
166	        r = harness.audit_faithfulness_primary(bad, g)
167	    finally:
168	        RA.set_entailment_judge(None)
169	    assert r.verdict == FAIL and r.faith_label == FAITH_UNREACHABLE
170	    assert RC_FAITH_UNREACHABLE in r.reason_codes
171	
172	
173	def test_faithfulness_uses_manifestation_bytes_not_card_span(graph_and_clean):
174	    """Sol §Faithfulness: resolved_span = manifestation.text[start:end], the VERIFIED bytes — never
175	    card['span']. A card whose display `span` was swapped to a lie but whose offsets still verify is judged
176	    against the real bytes, so the swap cannot launder the claim."""
177	    g, card = graph_and_clean
178	    poisoned = copy.deepcopy(card)
179	    poisoned['span'] = 'automation doubled worldwide causing mass unemployment'  # a lie in the DISPLAY field
180	    resolved = harness.resolve_verified_span(poisoned, g)
181	    assert resolved == card['span_raw']                 # the real bytes, not the swapped display span
182	
183	
184	# =================================================================================================
185	# Opus response schema validation (Sol Phase 2 step 6, Phase 3 step 5) — fail closed
186	# =================================================================================================
187	def test_valid_response_validates():
188	    v = _verdict('rid-1', numeric_applicable=True)
189	    assert harness.validate_opus_response(v, 'rid-1') is v
190	
191	
192	def test_wrong_audit_row_id_rejected():
193	    with pytest.raises(OpusResponseInvalid):
194	        harness.validate_opus_response(_verdict('rid-1'), 'rid-2')
195	
196	
197	def test_missing_numeric_subdimension_rejected():
198	    v = _verdict('rid-1', numeric_applicable=True)
199	    del v['numeric']['subdimensions']['modality']       # a numeric claim missing one subdimension
200	    with pytest.raises(OpusResponseInvalid):
201	        harness.validate_opus_response(v, 'rid-1')
202	
203	
204	def test_illegal_verdict_rejected():
205	    v = _verdict('rid-1')
206	    v['relevance']['verdict'] = 'DEFINITELY'
207	    with pytest.raises(OpusResponseInvalid):
208	        harness.validate_opus_response(v, 'rid-1')
209	
210	
211	def test_unknown_key_rejected():
212	    v = _verdict('rid-1')
213	    v['reasoning_trace'] = 'let me think step by step'   # no free-form reasoning persisted (Sol Phase 2)
214	    with pytest.raises(OpusResponseInvalid):
215	        harness.validate_opus_response(v, 'rid-1')
216	
217	
218	def test_illegal_disposition_rejected():
219	    v = _verdict('rid-1', disp='DELETE')                 # there is no DELETE disposition (Sol §4)
220	    with pytest.raises(OpusResponseInvalid):
221	        harness.validate_opus_response(v, 'rid-1')
222	
223	
224	# =================================================================================================
225	# Model-metadata enforcement — opus only, no cheaper fallback (Sol §"Efficient execution")
226	# =================================================================================================
227	def test_model_is_opus_true_for_opus_envelope():
228	    assert harness.model_is_opus(_envelope(_verdict('r'), model='claude-opus-4-8')) is True
229	
230	
231	def test_model_is_opus_false_for_cheaper_model():
232	    assert harness.model_is_opus(_envelope(_verdict('r'), model='claude-sonnet-4-5')) is False
233	    assert harness.model_is_opus(_envelope(_verdict('r'), model='glm-4.6')) is False
234	
235	
236	def test_model_is_opus_false_when_metadata_absent():
237	    assert harness.model_is_opus({'result': '{}'}) is False
238	
239	
240	def test_run_opus_pass_flags_unverified_model(graph_and_clean):
241	    g, card = graph_and_clean
242	    rec = _receipt(card, g)
243	    runner = _runner_for(_verdict(rec.audit_row_id), model='claude-sonnet-4-5')
244	    v = harness.run_opus_pass(card, g, question=QUESTION, contract_facets=FACETS,
245	                              det_receipt=rec, pass_label=PASS_A, runner=runner)
246	    assert v.model_verified is False
247	
248	
249	# =================================================================================================
250	# Fail-closed combination (Sol §Faithfulness, §Tier 3, §4)
251	# =================================================================================================
252	def _clean_faith():
253	    return harness.FaithfulnessReceipt(PASS, 'ENTAILED', [], '', 'primary')
254	
255	
256	def test_clean_card_two_agreeing_passes_keep_unchanged(graph_and_clean):
257	    g, card = graph_and_clean
258	    rec = _receipt(card, g)
259	    a = OpusVerdict(rec.audit_row_id, PASS_A, True, _verdict(rec.audit_row_id))
260	    b = OpusVerdict(rec.audit_row_id, PASS_B, True, _verdict(rec.audit_row_id))
261	    c = harness.combine_card_verdicts(rec, _clean_faith(), [], a, b)
262	    assert c.final == PASS and c.proposed_disposition == 'KEEP_UNCHANGED'
263	    assert c.needs_adjudication is False
264	
265	
266	def test_reportast_faith_fail_cannot_be_overridden_by_opus(graph_and_clean):
267	    """Sol §Faithfulness/§Tier 3: a report-AST FAIL is decisive; two PASSing Opus opinions cannot save
268	    the card."""
269	    g, card = graph_and_clean
270	    rec = _receipt(card, g)
271	    faith_fail = harness.FaithfulnessReceipt(FAIL, 'NOT_ENTAILED', [RC_FAITH_NOT_ENTAILED], 'x', 'primary')
272	    a = OpusVerdict(rec.audit_row_id, PASS_A, True, _verdict(rec.audit_row_id))  # both say KEEP
273	    b = OpusVerdict(rec.audit_row_id, PASS_B, True, _verdict(rec.audit_row_id))
274	    c = harness.combine_card_verdicts(rec, faith_fail, [], a, b)
275	    assert c.final == FAIL and c.proposed_disposition == 'QUARANTINE_CARD'
276	
277	
278	def test_disagreeing_passes_route_to_adjudication(graph_and_clean):
279	    g, card = graph_and_clean
280	    rec = _receipt(card, g)
281	    a = OpusVerdict(rec.audit_row_id, PASS_A, True, _verdict(rec.audit_row_id))
282	    b = OpusVerdict(rec.audit_row_id, PASS_B, True,
283	                    _verdict(rec.audit_row_id, rel=(FAIL, 'GENERIC_FILLER'), disp='QUARANTINE_CARD'))
284	    c = harness.combine_card_verdicts(rec, _clean_faith(), [], a, b)
285	    assert c.needs_adjudication is True and c.final == UNCERTAIN
286	    assert RC_OPUS_DISAGREE in c.reason_codes
287	
288	
289	def test_reportast_pass_but_opus_alleges_atom_routes_to_adjudication(graph_and_clean):
290	    g, card = graph_and_clean
291	    rec = _receipt(card, g)
292	    a = OpusVerdict(rec.audit_row_id, PASS_A, True, _verdict(rec.audit_row_id, faith=FAIL))
293	    b = OpusVerdict(rec.audit_row_id, PASS_B, True, _verdict(rec.audit_row_id, faith=FAIL))
294	    c = harness.combine_card_verdicts(rec, _clean_faith(), [], a, b)
295	    assert c.needs_adjudication is True and RC_OPUS_ALLEGES_ATOM in c.reason_codes
296	
297	
298	def test_missing_pass_fails_closed(graph_and_clean):
299	    g, card = graph_and_clean
300	    rec = _receipt(card, g)
301	    a = OpusVerdict(rec.audit_row_id, PASS_A, True, _verdict(rec.audit_row_id))
302	    c = harness.combine_card_verdicts(rec, _clean_faith(), [], a, None)
303	    assert c.final == UNCERTAIN and RC_OPUS_TRANSPORT in c.reason_codes
304	
305	
306	def test_unverified_model_fails_closed(graph_and_clean):
307	    g, card = graph_and_clean
308	    rec = _receipt(card, g)
309	    a = OpusVerdict(rec.audit_row_id, PASS_A, False, _verdict(rec.audit_row_id))
310	    b = OpusVerdict(rec.audit_row_id, PASS_B, False, _verdict(rec.audit_row_id))
311	    c = harness.combine_card_verdicts(rec, _clean_faith(), [], a, b)
312	    assert c.final == UNCERTAIN and RC_OPUS_MODEL in c.reason_codes
313	
314	
315	def test_both_passes_agree_on_relevance_fail(graph_and_clean):
316	    g, card = graph_and_clean
317	    rec = _receipt(card, g)
318	    bad = _verdict(rec.audit_row_id, rel=(FAIL, 'OTHER_QUESTION'), disp='QUARANTINE_CARD')
319	    a = OpusVerdict(rec.audit_row_id, PASS_A, True, bad)
320	    b = OpusVerdict(rec.audit_row_id, PASS_B, True, bad)
321	    c = harness.combine_card_verdicts(rec, _clean_faith(), [], a, b)
322	    assert c.final == FAIL and RC_OPUS_RELEVANCE in c.reason_codes
323	
324	
325	# =================================================================================================
326	# Tier 3 adjudication floors (Sol §Tier 3)
327	# =================================================================================================
328	def test_adjudicator_cannot_override_reportast_fail(graph_and_clean):
329	    g, card = graph_and_clean
330	    rec = _receipt(card, g)
331	    faith_fail = harness.FaithfulnessReceipt(FAIL, 'NOT_ENTAILED', [RC_FAITH_NOT_ENTAILED], 'x', 'primary')
332	    a = OpusVerdict(rec.audit_row_id, PASS_A, True, _verdict(rec.audit_row_id))
333	    b = OpusVerdict(rec.audit_row_id, PASS_B, True, _verdict(rec.audit_row_id))
334	    runner = _runner_for(_verdict(rec.audit_row_id, disp='KEEP_UNCHANGED'))  # adjudicator says keep
335	    c = harness.run_adjudication(card, g, rec, faith_fail, [], a, b,
336	                                 question=QUESTION, contract_facets=FACETS, runner=runner)
337	    assert c.final == FAIL and c.proposed_disposition == 'QUARANTINE_CARD'
338	
339	
340	def test_adjudicator_resolves_false_positive_to_keep(graph_and_clean):
341	    g, card = graph_and_clean
342	    rec = _receipt(card, g)
343	    a = OpusVerdict(rec.audit_row_id, PASS_A, True, _verdict(rec.audit_row_id))
344	    b = OpusVerdict(rec.audit_row_id, PASS_B, True,
345	                    _verdict(rec.audit_row_id, rel=(FAIL, 'GENERIC_FILLER'), disp='QUARANTINE_CARD'))
346	    runner = _runner_for(_verdict(rec.audit_row_id, disp='KEEP_UNCHANGED'))
347	    c = harness.run_adjudication(card, g, rec, _clean_faith(), [], a, b,
348	                                 question=QUESTION, contract_facets=FACETS, runner=runner)
349	    assert c.final == PASS and c.proposed_disposition == 'KEEP_UNCHANGED'
350	
351	
352	def test_adjudicator_response_must_prove_opus(graph_and_clean):
353	    g, card = graph_and_clean
354	    rec = _receipt(card, g)
355	    a = OpusVerdict(rec.audit_row_id, PASS_A, True, _verdict(rec.audit_row_id))
356	    b = OpusVerdict(rec.audit_row_id, PASS_B, True, _verdict(rec.audit_row_id))
357	    runner = _runner_for(_verdict(rec.audit_row_id), model='claude-haiku-4-5')
358	    c = harness.run_adjudication(card, g, rec, _clean_faith(), [], a, b,
359	                                 question=QUESTION, contract_facets=FACETS, runner=runner)
360	    assert c.final == UNCERTAIN and RC_OPUS_MODEL in c.reason_codes
361	
362	
363	# =================================================================================================
364	# End-to-end audit_card with the injected transport (Sol §7 per-card slice)
365	# =================================================================================================
366	def test_audit_card_happy_path_keeps_clean_card(graph_and_clean):
367	    g, card = graph_and_clean
368	    rec = _receipt(card, g)
369	    runner = _runner_for(_verdict(rec.audit_row_id))
370	    RA.set_entailment_judge(lambda clause, span: ('ENTAILED', 'ok'))
371	    try:
372	        c = harness.audit_card(card, g, rec, question=QUESTION, contract_facets=FACETS, runner=runner)
373	    finally:
374	        RA.set_entailment_judge(None)
375	    assert c.final == PASS and c.proposed_disposition == 'KEEP_UNCHANGED'
376	
377	
378	def test_audit_card_transport_failure_fails_closed(graph_and_clean):
379	    g, card = graph_and_clean
380	    rec = _receipt(card, g)
381	
382	    def dead_runner(_prompt, _schema):
383	        raise OpusUnavailable('opus transport down')
384	
385	    RA.set_entailment_judge(lambda clause, span: ('ENTAILED', 'ok'))
386	    try:
387	        c = harness.audit_card(card, g, rec, question=QUESTION, contract_facets=FACETS, runner=dead_runner)
388	    finally:
389	        RA.set_entailment_judge(None)
390	    assert c.final == UNCERTAIN and c.proposed_disposition == 'QUARANTINE_CARD'
391	    assert RC_OPUS_TRANSPORT in c.reason_codes
392	
393	
394	# =================================================================================================
395	# Generality / metamorphic (Sol Phase 8): renaming the subject changes no verdict
396	# =================================================================================================
397	def test_question_and_facet_rename_change_no_verdict(graph_and_clean):
398	    g, card = graph_and_clean
399	    rec = _receipt(card, g)
400	    runner = _runner_for(_verdict(rec.audit_row_id))
401	    RA.set_entailment_judge(lambda clause, span: ('ENTAILED', 'ok'))
402	    try:
403	        c1 = harness.audit_card(card, g, rec, question=QUESTION, contract_facets=FACETS, runner=runner)
404	        # a clinical corpus: different subject words, structurally identical card
405	        c2 = harness.audit_card(card, g, rec, question='What is the treatment effect on mortality?',
406	                                contract_facets=['oncology', 'survival'], runner=runner)
407	    finally:
408	        RA.set_entailment_judge(None)
409	    assert c1.final == c2.final == PASS
410	    assert c1.proposed_disposition == c2.proposed_disposition
411	
412	
413	def test_no_task_literal_in_prompt(graph_and_clean):
414	    """Sol Phase 8 / §Generality: the production prompt carries no task-72/DOI/venue literal — only
415	    injected values. Build a packet+prompt and assert the injected question is what parametrizes it."""
416	    g, card = graph_and_clean
417	    rec = _receipt(card, g)
418	    schema = harness.opus_response_json_schema()
419	    packet = harness.build_opus_packet(card, g, question='SENTINEL_QUESTION',
420	                                       contract_facets=['SENTINEL_FACET'], det_receipt=rec)
421	    prompt = harness.build_opus_prompt(packet, schema)
422	    assert 'SENTINEL_QUESTION' in prompt and 'SENTINEL_FACET' in prompt
423	    # the resolved span injected is the exact verified bytes
424	    assert card['span_raw'] in prompt
425
